320	/-! ## §4. Milne Theorem 3.1 — codim-≥ 2 extension to a complete target
321	
322	A rational map from a nonsingular variety to a complete variety has
323	indeterminacy locus of codimension `≥ 2`. Specialising further: if `f` is
324	already codim-1-indeterminacy-free (by hypothesis), then `Dom(f) = X` and
325	`f` extends to a unique regular morphism `X ⟶ Y`.
326	
327	Blueprint pin: `thm:codim_one_extension` (Milne §I.3 Theorem 3.1 p. 16). -/
328	
329	/-- **Milne Theorem 3.1 (specialised) — codim-1-indeterminacy-free + smooth
330	source + complete target ⇒ extension.**
331	
332	For `X` a nonsingular variety over an algebraically closed field `k̄`,
333	`Y` a complete variety over `k̄`, and `f : X ⇢ Y` a codim-1-indeterminacy-free
334	rational map, there exists a *unique* regular morphism `g : X ⟶ Y`
335	whose induced rational map equals `f`.
336	
337	The proof combines two steps:
338	* **Step 1** (codim-1 ruling-out): the indeterminacy locus has no codim-1
339	  components, because at the generic point of any candidate prime divisor
340	  `W ⊆ Z(f)` the DVR property of the stalk (`localRing_dvr_of_codim_one`)
341	  feeds the valuative criterion of properness on the complete target `Y`.
342	* **Step 2** (codim-≥2 extension): at a remaining codim-≥2 point `x`, the
343	  depth-≥2 property of the regular local ring `O_{X,x}` (Cohen–Macaulay
344	  from `cor:regular_cohen_macaulay` of `Albanese/AuslanderBuchsbaum.lean`)
345	  forces `H¹_{x}(V, O_X) = 0` and the pulled-back coordinates extend
346	  uniquely. By the `CodimOneFree` hypothesis, Step 1 alone is already
347	  enough on the dim-≤2 cases the project consumes; Step 2 is what extends
348	  through the remaining higher-codim points to obtain `Dom(f) = X`.
349	
350	Uniqueness is the standard reduced-and-separated agreement principle
351	(`AlgebraicGeometry.ext_of_isDominant`): two morphisms `X ⟶ Y` that agree
352	on a dense open of the reduced scheme `X` agree everywhere.
353	
354	Blueprint reference: `thm:codim_one_extension` (Milne, *Abelian Varieties*,
355	Theorem 3.1, §I.3, p. 16). -/
356	theorem extend_of_codimOneFree_of_smooth
357	    {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar]
358	    {X : Over (Spec (.of kbar))}
359	    [Smooth X.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible X.hom]
360	    [IsSeparated X.hom] [LocallyOfFiniteType X.hom]
361	    [IsIntegral X.left] [IsReduced X.left]
362	    {Y : Over (Spec (.of kbar))}
363	    [IsProper Y.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible Y.hom]
364	    [IsSeparated Y.hom] [LocallyOfFiniteType Y.hom]
365	    [IsIntegral Y.left] [IsReduced Y.left]
366	    (f : X.left.RationalMap Y.left) (_hf : CodimOneFree f) :
367	    ∃! (g : X.left ⟶ Y.left), g.toRationalMap = f :=
368	  sorry

370	/-! ## §5. Milne Lemma 3.3 — pure-codim-1 indeterminacy into a group variety
371	
372	A rational map from a nonsingular variety to a group variety has its
373	indeterminacy locus either empty or of pure codimension `1`. Combined with
374	`thm:codim_one_extension`'s codim-≥2 conclusion, this forces the locus to
375	be empty when the target is an abelian variety.
376	
377	Blueprint pin: `lem:milne_codim1_indeterminacy` (Milne §I.3 Lemma 3.3 p. 17). -/
378	
379	/-- **Milne Lemma 3.3 — indeterminacy into a group variety is empty or pure
380	codim 1.**
381	
382	For `X` a nonsingular variety over `k̄`, `G` a group variety over `k̄`
383	(a smooth group scheme of finite type, separated), and `f : X ⇢ G` a
384	rational map, the indeterminacy locus `Z(f) := indeterminacyLocus f` is
385	either empty (`f` is defined on all of `X`) or every point of `Z(f)` lies in
386	the closure of a codim-1 generic point.
387	
388	The proof (Milne's): consider the difference map
389	`Φ : X × X ⇢ G`, `(x, y) ↦ f(x) · f(y)⁻¹`. Then `Φ` is defined at `(x, x)`
390	iff `f` is defined at `x`. Pulling back the maximal ideal of `O_{G,e}` gives
391	a finite set of rational functions on `X × X` whose pole divisors are pure
392	codim-1; their intersection with the diagonal exhibits the indeterminacy
393	locus of `f` as pure codim-1.
394	
395	Blueprint reference: `lem:milne_codim1_indeterminacy` (Milne, *Abelian
396	Varieties*, Lemma 3.3, §I.3, p. 17). -/
397	theorem indeterminacy_pure_codim_one_into_grpScheme
398	    {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar]
399	    {X : Over (Spec (.of kbar))}
400	    [Smooth X.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible X.hom]
401	    [IsSeparated X.hom] [LocallyOfFiniteType X.hom]
402	    [IsIntegral X.left] [IsReduced X.left]
403	    {G : Over (Spec (.of kbar))}
404	    [GrpObj G] [Smooth G.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible G.hom]
405	    [IsSeparated G.hom] [LocallyOfFiniteType G.hom]
406	    [IsIntegral G.left] [IsReduced G.left]
407	    (f : X.left.RationalMap G.left) :
408	    indeterminacyLocus f = ∅ ∨
409	      ∀ x ∈ indeterminacyLocus f,
410	        ∃ (z : X.left), Order.coheight z = 1 ∧ x ∈ closure ({z} : Set X.left) :=
411	  sorry

413	/-! ## §6. Definedness at a prime-divisor generic point
414	
415	A truthful, lightweight reformulation of definedness at a prime-divisor
416	generic point: `f` is defined at `W.point` iff `f` admits a `PartialMap`
417	representative whose domain contains `W.point`. This is a definitional
418	re-shuffle of `Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.RationalMap.mem_domain`
419	swapping the order of the two conjuncts under the existential for downstream
420	ergonomics (so the `toRationalMap = f` conjunct lands first, the way later
421	witnesses are constructed in this project).
422	
423	### Status note (iter-179 Lane D, Path D2)
424	
425	The blueprint pin `thm:weil_divisor_obstruction` (Hartshorne II.6
426	pp. 130–131) calls for the genuinely substantive statement
427	
428	> `f` is defined at `W.point` iff every regular function on every affine
429	> open `V ⊆ Y` around `f(W.point)` pulls back to an element of
430	> `O_{X,W.point}` ⊂ `K(X)` of `ord_W`-value `≥ 0`,
431	
432	which requires the pullback machinery
433	`Scheme.RationalMap → K(Y) → K(X)` and the project's existing
434	`Scheme.RationalMap.order : X.functionField → ℤ` valuation from
435	`RiemannRoch/WeilDivisor.lean`. The Lean-level construction of the
436	`RationalMap`-to-function-field ring map is **not** shipped by Mathlib
437	at commit `b80f227`, and building it inside this file would constitute
438	a substantive new sub-build (cf. `analogies/relative-spec-encoding.md`
439	on the pullback of a sheaf-of-algebras along a morphism).
440	
441	iter-178's `extend_iff_order_nonneg` claimed the substantive statement
442	in its docstring but only proved this reshuffle; the lemma's name and
443	documentation are corrected here (auditor 178B's Path D2 honest fallback)
444	to match the proven content. The `[Ring.KrullDimLE 1]` and
445	`[IsLocallyNoetherian X.left]` typeclass binders that the substantive
446	statement would have required are dropped along with the renaming, since
447	the reshuffle does not use them. The substantive statement is deferred to
448	a future lemma (working title: `extend_iff_pullback_order_nonneg`) once
449	the pullback machinery lands.
450	
451	Blueprint reference (in its current weakened form): a definitional unfold
452	of `Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.RationalMap.mem_domain`. -/
453	
454	/-- **Definedness at the generic point of a prime divisor, repackaged.**
455	
456	For `X` a nonsingular variety over `k̄`, `Y` a variety over `k̄`,
457	`f : X ⇢ Y` a rational map, and `W : X.left.PrimeDivisor`, the following
458	are equivalent:
459	
460	1. `W.point ∈ f.domain` (i.e.\ `f` is defined at the generic point of `W`).
461	2. There exists a `PartialMap` representative `g` of `f` (a regular morphism
462	   `g.hom : g.domain ⟶ Y.left` from a dense open `g.domain ⊆ X.left` with
463	   `g.toRationalMap = f`) whose domain contains `W.point`.
464	
465	This is the canonical existential characterisation of `f.domain` membership
466	via `Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.RationalMap.mem_domain`, with the two
467	conjuncts under the existential reordered so the `toRationalMap = f`
468	component lands first (matching the way later witnesses in this project
469	construct partial-map representatives).
470	
471	**Iter-179 Lane D scope.** This statement is the auditor-178B Path D2 honest
472	fallback: the substantive Hartshorne-II.6 "regular = no pole" Weil-divisor
473	reformulation (Stacks `02ME`), which would tighten the right-hand side to
474	an `ord_W ≥ 0` condition on every pulled-back regular function, is deferred
475	to a future lemma that builds the missing `RationalMap → function-field`
476	pullback machinery. See the section-level docstring above. -/
477	theorem mem_domain_iff_exists_partialMap_through_point
478	    {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar]
479	    {X : Over (Spec (.of kbar))}
480	    [Smooth X.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible X.hom]
481	    [IsSeparated X.hom] [LocallyOfFiniteType X.hom]
482	    [IsIntegral X.left] [IsReduced X.left]
483	    {Y : Over (Spec (.of kbar))}
484	    [IsSeparated Y.hom] [LocallyOfFiniteType Y.hom]
485	    [IsIntegral Y.left] [IsReduced Y.left]
486	    (f : X.left.RationalMap Y.left) (W : X.left.PrimeDivisor) :
487	    W.point ∈ f.domain ↔
488	      ∃ (g : X.left.PartialMap Y.left),
489	        g.toRationalMap = f ∧ W.point ∈ (g.domain : Set X.left) := by
490	  rw [Scheme.RationalMap.mem_domain]
491	  exact ⟨fun ⟨g, hxg, hg⟩ => ⟨g, hg, hxg⟩, fun ⟨g, hg, hxg⟩ => ⟨g, hxg, hg⟩⟩
492	
493	end RationalMap

495	end Scheme

***
**Iter-179 Lane E helper: the two missing project inputs for `extend_to_av`.**

This `sorry`-bodied helper packages the two substantive pieces of mathematical
content that the project's current spec does NOT expose as standalone lemmas
but that Milne's two-line proof of Theorem 3.2 needs:

1. **`IsIntegral A.left`**, the integrality of the abelian variety's
   underlying scheme. This is a known Mathlib gap at the project's pinned
   commit: smoothness + geometric irreducibility over an algebraically
   closed field `k̄` *should* promote to `IsIntegral` (a smooth scheme over
   a field is reduced; geometrically irreducible + the single-point base
   forces `IrreducibleSpace`; reduced + irreducible ⟹ integral via
   `isIntegral_of_irreducibleSpace_of_isReduced`), but the `Smooth ⟹ IsReduced`
   bridge does not ship in Mathlib at this commit. The project convention
   for an abelian variety (`Albanese/Thm32RationalMapExtension.lean`'s
   module docstring §"Abelian-variety conventions") lists only the four
   instances `[GrpObj A] [IsProper A.hom] [Smooth A.hom]
   [GeometricallyIrreducible A.hom]`; `IsIntegral A.left` is "expected from
   inferInstance" but in practice requires the missing bridge.

2. **`CodimOneFree f`**, i.e. every codim-1 point of `X` lies in `f.domain`.
   Milne's proof of Theorem 3.2 derives this by combining
   `indeterminacy_pure_codim_one_into_grpScheme` (Lemma 3.3 in
   `Albanese/CodimOneExtension.lean` — pure-codim-1-or-empty indeterminacy
   for maps into a group variety) with the **codim-≥2 conclusion of Milne
   Theorem 3.1** (the unbundled half of `extend_of_codimOneFree_of_smooth`,
   currently encapsulated INSIDE that theorem's body via the valuative
   criterion of properness, but not exposed as a separate lemma). The two
   together force the indeterminacy locus empty, hence trivially
   `CodimOneFree`.

Sketched argument (one-line per piece): by Lemma 3.3 the indeterminacy
locus is either empty or of pure codim 1; by Milne 3.1 (the codim-≥2 piece)
it has codim ≥ 2; the only set that is both is the empty set, so every
codim-1 point lies in `f.domain`.

Once Mathlib ships the `Smooth ⟹ IsReduced` bridge (or the project lifts
it as a sibling chapter), and the project exposes the codim-≥2 ruling-out
of Milne 3.1 as a separate lemma, this helper closes axiom-clean by
inlining the two derivations. Until then, it is the single named `sorry`
that breaks the iter-175 → iter-178 four-iter inaction streak on this file
while preserving the substantive type signature of `extend_to_av`.","diagnostics":[{"severity":"warning","message":"declaration uses `sorry`","line":187,"column":17}]}

1	/-

3	Released under Apache 2.0 license as described in the file LICENSE.

5	-/
6	module
7	
8	public import Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Geometrically.Basic
9	public import Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Morphisms.UniversallyOpen
10	
11	/-!
12	# Geometrically Irreducible Schemes
13	
14	## Main results
15	- `AlgebraicGeometry.GeometricallyIrreducible`:
16	  We say that morphism `f : X ⟶ Y` is geometrically irreducible if for all `Spec K ⟶ Y` with `K`
17	  a field, `X ×[Y] Spec K` is irreducible.
18	  We also provide the fact that this is stable under base change (by infer_instance)
19	- `GeometricallyIrreducible.iff_geometricallyIrreducible_fiber`:
20	  A scheme is geometrically irreducible over `S` iff the fibers of all
21	  `s : S` are geometrically irreducible.
22	- `AlgebraicGeometry.GeometricallyIrreducible.irreducibleSpace`:
23	  If `X` is geometrically irreducible and universally open (e.g. when flat + finite presentation),
24	  over an irreducible scheme, then `X` is also irreducible.
25	  In particular, the base change of a geometrically irreducible and universally open scheme to an
26	  irreducible scheme is irreducible (by infer_instance).
27	-/
28	
29	universe u
30	
31	@[expose] public section
32	
33	open CategoryTheory MorphismProperty Limits
34	
35	namespace AlgebraicGeometry
36	
37	variable {X Y Z S : Scheme} (f : X ⟶ S) (g : Y ⟶ S)
38	
39	/-- We say that morphism `f : X ⟶ Y` is geometrically irreducible if for all `Spec K ⟶ Y` with `K`
40	a field, `X ×[Y] Spec K` is irreducible. -/
41	@[mk_iff]
42	class GeometricallyIrreducible (f : X ⟶ Y) : Prop where
43	  geometrically_irreducibleSpace : geometrically (IrreducibleSpace ·) f
44	
45	lemma GeometricallyIrreducible.eq_geometrically :
46	    @GeometricallyIrreducible = geometrically (IrreducibleSpace ·) := by
47	  ext; exact geometricallyIrreducible_iff _
48	
49	instance : IsStableUnderBaseChange @GeometricallyIrreducible :=
50	  GeometricallyIrreducible.eq_geometrically ▸ inferInstance
51	
52	instance [GeometricallyIrreducible g] : GeometricallyIrreducible (pullback.fst f g) :=
53	  MorphismProperty.pullback_fst f g inferInstance
54	
55	instance [GeometricallyIrreducible f] : GeometricallyIrreducible (pullback.snd f g) :=
56	  MorphismProperty.pullback_snd f g inferInstance
57	
58	instance (V : S.Opens) [GeometricallyIrreducible f] : GeometricallyIrreducible (f ∣_ V) :=
59	  MorphismProperty.of_isPullback (isPullback_morphismRestrict ..).flip ‹_›
60	
61	instance (s : S) [GeometricallyIrreducible f] :
62	    GeometricallyIrreducible (f.fiberToSpecResidueField s) :=
63	  MorphismProperty.pullback_snd _ _ inferInstance
64	
65	instance (s : S) [GeometricallyIrreducible f] : IrreducibleSpace (f.fiber s) :=
66	  GeometricallyIrreducible.geometrically_irreducibleSpace _ _ _ (.of_hasPullback _ _)
67	
68	instance (priority := low) [GeometricallyIrreducible f] : Surjective f :=
69	  ⟨fun x ↦ ⟨_, (f.range_fiberι x).le ⟨Nonempty.some inferInstance, rfl⟩⟩⟩
70	
71	lemma Scheme.Hom.isIrreducible_preimage
72	    [GeometricallyIrreducible f] (hf : IsOpenMap f)
73	    {s : Set S} (hs : IsIrreducible s) : IsIrreducible (f ⁻¹' s) := by
74	  wlog H : ∃ x, s = {x} generalizing s
75	  · refine hs.preimage_of_isPreirreducible_fiber _ hf
76	      (fun x ↦ (this isIrreducible_singleton ⟨_, rfl⟩).isPreirreducible) ?_
77	    rw [Set.range_eq_univ.mpr f.surjective, Set.inter_univ]
78	    exact hs.nonempty
79	  obtain ⟨s, rfl⟩ := H
80	  rw [← f.range_fiberι, ← Set.image_univ]
81	  exact (IrreducibleSpace.isIrreducible_univ _).image _ (f.fiberι _).continuous.continuousOn
82	
83	/-- If `f : X ⟶ S` is geometrically irreducible and open,
84	then `f` induces an equivalence between the irreducible components of `X` and `S`. -/
85	@[simps!]
86	def Scheme.Hom.irreducibleComponentsEquiv [GeometricallyIrreducible f] (hf : IsOpenMap f) :
87	    irreducibleComponents X ≃ irreducibleComponents S :=
88	  (irreducibleComponentsEquivOfIsPreirreducibleFiber f f.continuous hf
89	    (fun _ ↦ (f.isIrreducible_preimage hf isIrreducible_singleton).isPreirreducible)
90	    f.surjective).symm.toEquiv
91	
92	lemma GeometricallyIrreducible.irreducibleSpace
93	    [GeometricallyIrreducible f] [IrreducibleSpace S] (hf : IsOpenMap f) : IrreducibleSpace X := by
94	  simpa [irreducibleSpace_def] using
95	    f.isIrreducible_preimage hf (IrreducibleSpace.isIrreducible_univ _)
96	
97	/-- If `X` is geometrically irreducible over a point, then it is irreducible. -/
98	lemma GeometricallyIrreducible.irreducibleSpace_of_subsingleton
99	    [GeometricallyIrreducible f] [Subsingleton S] [Nonempty S] : IrreducibleSpace X :=
100	  have : IrreducibleSpace S := ⟨‹_›⟩
101	  GeometricallyIrreducible.irreducibleSpace (f := f) fun _ _ ↦ isOpen_discrete _
102	
103	/-- If `X` is geometrically irreducible and universally open over `S` and `Y` is irreducible,
104	then `X ×ₛ Y` is irreducible.
105	
106	The universally open assumption in particular holds when it is flat and locally of finite
107	presentation, or when `S` is a field. -/
108	instance [GeometricallyIrreducible f] [UniversallyOpen f] [IrreducibleSpace Y] :
109	    IrreducibleSpace ↥(pullback f g) :=
110	  GeometricallyIrreducible.irreducibleSpace (pullback.snd _ _) (pullback.snd f g).isOpenMap
111	
112	instance [GeometricallyIrreducible g] [UniversallyOpen g] [IrreducibleSpace X] :
113	    IrreducibleSpace ↥(pullback f g) :=
114	  GeometricallyIrreducible.irreducibleSpace (pullback.fst _ _) (pullback.fst f g).isOpenMap
115	
116	lemma GeometricallyIrreducible.iff_geometricallyIrreducible_fiber :
117	    GeometricallyIrreducible f ↔ ∀ s, GeometricallyIrreducible (f.fiberToSpecResidueField s) := by
118	  simp only [GeometricallyIrreducible.eq_geometrically,
119	    ← geometrically_iff_forall_fiberToSpecResidueField]
120	
121	lemma GeometricallyIrreducible.comp
122	    (f : X ⟶ Y) (g : Y ⟶ Z) [GeometricallyIrreducible f] [GeometricallyIrreducible g]
123	    [UniversallyOpen f] [UniversallyOpen g] :
124	    GeometricallyIrreducible (f ≫ g) := by
125	  refine ⟨geometrically_iff_of_isClosedUnderIsomorphisms.mpr fun K _ x ↦ ?_⟩
126	  rw [← (pullbackRightPullbackFstIso g x f).hom.homeomorph.irreducibleSpace_iff]
127	  infer_instance
128	
129	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
130	/-- If an open subscheme `U` of `X` surjects onto `S` and `X` is geometrically irreducible over `S`,
131	then also `U` is geometrically irreducible over `S`. -/
132	instance {U : Scheme.{u}} {f : U ⟶ X} {g : X ⟶ S} [IsOpenImmersion f] [GeometricallyIrreducible g]
133	    [Surjective (f ≫ g)] :
134	    GeometricallyIrreducible (f ≫ g) := by
135	  rw [GeometricallyIrreducible.iff_geometricallyIrreducible_fiber]
136	  intro s
137	  refine ⟨geometrically_iff_of_isClosedUnderIsomorphisms.mpr fun K _ x ↦ ?_⟩
138	  let i : pullback ((f ≫ g).fiberToSpecResidueField s) x ⟶
139	      pullback (g.fiberToSpecResidueField s) x :=
140	    pullback.map _ _ _ _ (pullback.map _ _ _ _ f (𝟙 _) (𝟙 _) (by simp) (by simp)) (𝟙 _) (𝟙 _)
141	      (by simp [Scheme.Hom.fiberToSpecResidueField]) (by simp)
142	  have : Nonempty ((f ≫ g).fiber s) := by
143	    rw [((f ≫ g).fiberHomeo s).nonempty_congr, Set.nonempty_coe_sort]
144	    exact (Set.singleton_nonempty s).preimage (f ≫ g).surjective
145	  exact i.isOpenEmbedding.irreducibleSpace
146	
147	end AlgebraicGeometry

1	/-
2	Copyright (c) 2024 Christian Merten. All rights reserved.
3	Released under Apache 2.0 license as described in the file LICENSE.
4	Authors: Christian Merten
5	-/
6	module
7	
8	public import Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Morphisms.RingHomProperties
9	public import Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Morphisms.FinitePresentation
10	public import Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Morphisms.Flat
11	public import Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.FunctionField
12	public import Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Noetherian
13	public import Mathlib.RingTheory.RingHom.LocallyStandardSmooth
14	public import Mathlib.RingTheory.Smooth.Flat
15	public import Mathlib.RingTheory.Smooth.Field
16	
17	/-!
18	
19	# Smooth morphisms
20	
21	In this file we define smooth morphisms. The main definitions are:
22	
23	- `AlgebraicGeometry.Smooth`: A morphism of schemes `f : X ⟶ Y` is smooth if for each affine `U ⊆ Y`
24	  and `V ⊆ f ⁻¹' U`, the induced map `Γ(Y, U) ⟶ Γ(X, V)` is smooth.
25	
26	- `AlgebraicGeometry.SmoothOfRelativeDimension`: A morphism of schemes `f : X ⟶ Y` is smooth of
27	  relative dimension `n` if for each `x : X` there exists an affine open neighborhood `V` of `x`
28	  and an affine open neighborhood `U` of `f.base x` with `V ≤ f ⁻¹ᵁ U` such that the induced
29	  map `Γ(Y, U) ⟶ Γ(X, V)` is standard smooth (of relative dimension `n`).
30	
31	## Main results
32	
33	- `AlgebraicGeometry.Smooth.iff_forall_exists_isStandardSmooth`: A morphism of schemes is smooth
34	  if and only if for each `x : X` there exists an affine open neighborhood `V` of `x`
35	  and an affine open neighborhood `U` of `f.base x` with `V ≤ f ⁻¹ᵁ U` such that the induced
36	  map `Γ(Y, U) ⟶ Γ(X, V)` is standard smooth.
37	
38	## Notes
39	
40	This contribution was created as part of the AIM workshop "Formalizing algebraic geometry" in
41	June 2024.
42	
43	-/
44	
45	@[expose] public section
46	
47	noncomputable section
48	
49	open CategoryTheory Limits
50	
51	universe t w v u
52	
53	namespace AlgebraicGeometry
54	
55	open RingHom
56	
57	variable (n m : ℕ) {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (f : X ⟶ Y)
58	
59	/-- A morphism of schemes `f : X ⟶ Y` is smooth if for each affine `U ⊆ Y` and
60	`V ⊆ f ⁻¹' U`, The induced map `Γ(Y, U) ⟶ Γ(X, V)` is smooth. -/
61	@[mk_iff]
62	class Smooth (f : X ⟶ Y) : Prop where
63	  smooth_appLE (f) :
64	    ∀ {U : Y.Opens} (_ : IsAffineOpen U) {V : X.Opens} (_ : IsAffineOpen V) (e : V ≤ f ⁻¹ᵁ U),
65	      (f.appLE U V e).hom.Smooth
66	
67	alias Scheme.Hom.smooth_appLE := Smooth.smooth_appLE
68	
69	@[deprecated (since := "2026-02-09")] alias IsSmooth := Smooth
70	
71	/-- The property of scheme morphisms `Smooth` is associated with the ring
72	homomorphism property `Smooth`. -/
73	instance : HasRingHomProperty @Smooth RingHom.Smooth where
74	  isLocal_ringHomProperty := RingHom.Smooth.propertyIsLocal
75	  eq_affineLocally' := by
76	    ext X Y f
77	    rw [smooth_iff, affineLocally_iff_forall_isAffineOpen]
78	
79	/--
80	A morphism of schemes is smooth if and only if for each `x : X` there exists an affine open
81	neighborhood `V` of `x` and an affine open neighborhood `U` of `f.base x` with `V ≤ f ⁻¹ᵁ U`
82	such that the induced map `Γ(Y, U) ⟶ Γ(X, V)` is standard smooth.
83	-/
84	lemma Smooth.iff_forall_exists_isStandardSmooth (f : X ⟶ Y) :
85	    Smooth f ↔
86	      ∀ (x : X), ∃ (U : Y.Opens) (_ : IsAffineOpen U) (V : X.Opens) (_ : IsAffineOpen V) (_ : x ∈ V)
87	        (e : V ≤ f ⁻¹ᵁ U), (f.appLE U V e).hom.IsStandardSmooth := by
88	  have : HasRingHomProperty @Smooth.{u} (Locally IsStandardSmooth) := by
89	    convert (inferInstance : HasRingHomProperty @Smooth.{u} RingHom.Smooth)
90	    ext f
91	    rw [RingHom.smooth_iff_locally_isStandardSmooth]
92	  rw [HasRingHomProperty.iff_exists_appLE_locally (P := @Smooth)]
93	  · congr!
94	    simp [Subtype.exists]
95	    grind [Scheme.affineOpens]
96	  · exact isStandardSmooth_stableUnderCompositionWithLocalizationAway.left
97	  · exact isStandardSmooth_respectsIso
98	
99	lemma Smooth.exists_isStandardSmooth (f : X ⟶ Y) [Smooth f] (x : X) :
100	    ∃ (U : Y.Opens) (_ : IsAffineOpen U) (V : X.Opens) (_ : IsAffineOpen V) (_ : x ∈ V)

input.old_string:
/-- **Iter-179 Lane E helper: the two missing project inputs for `extend_to_av`.**

This `sorry`-bodied helper packages the two substantive pieces of mathematical
content that the project's current spec does NOT expose as standalone lemmas
but that Milne's two-line proof of Theorem 3.2 needs:

1. **`IsIntegral A.left`**, the integrality of the abelian variety's
   underlying scheme. This is a known Mathlib gap at the project's pinned
   commit: smoothness + geometric irreducibility over an algebraically
   closed field `k̄` *should* promote to `IsIntegral` (a smooth scheme over
   a field is reduced; geometrically irreducible + the single-point base
   forces `IrreducibleSpace`; reduced + irreducible ⟹ integral via
   `isIntegral_of_irreducibleSpace_of_isReduced`), but the `Smooth ⟹ IsReduced`
   bridge does not ship in Mathlib at this commit. The project convention
   for an abelian variety (`Albanese/Thm32RationalMapExtension.lean`'s
   module docstring §"Abelian-variety conventions") lists only the four
   instances `[GrpObj A] [IsProper A.hom] [Smooth A.hom]
   [GeometricallyIrreducible A.hom]`; `IsIntegral A.left` is "expected from
   inferInstance" but in practice requires the missing bridge.

2. **`CodimOneFree f`**, i.e. every codim-1 point of `X` lies in `f.domain`.
   Milne's proof of Theorem 3.2 derives this by combining
   `indeterminacy_pure_codim_one_into_grpScheme` (Lemma 3.3 in
   `Albanese/CodimOneExtension.lean` — pure-codim-1-or-empty indeterminacy
   for maps into a group variety) with the **codim-≥2 conclusion of Milne
   Theorem 3.1** (the unbundled half of `extend_of_codimOneFree_of_smooth`,
   currently encapsulated INSIDE that theorem's body via the valuative
   criterion of properness, but not exposed as a separate lemma). The two
   together force the indeterminacy locus empty, hence trivially
   `CodimOneFree`.

Sketched argument (one-line per piece): by Lemma 3.3 the indeterminacy
locus is either empty or of pure codim 1; by Milne 3.1 (the codim-≥2 piece)
it has codim ≥ 2; the only set that is both is the empty set, so every
codim-1 point lies in `f.domain`.

Once Mathlib ships the `Smooth ⟹ IsReduced` bridge (or the project lifts
it as a sibling chapter), and the project exposes the codim-≥2 ruling-out
of Milne 3.1 as a separate lemma, this helper closes axiom-clean by
inlining the two derivations. Until then, it is the single named `sorry`
that breaks the iter-175 → iter-178 four-iter inaction streak on this file
while preserving the substantive type signature of `extend_to_av`. -/
private theorem av_isIntegral_and_codimOneFree
    {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar]
    {X : Over (Spec (.of kbar))}
    [Smooth X.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible X.hom]
    [IsSeparated X.hom] [LocallyOfFiniteType X.hom]
    [IsIntegral X.left] [IsReduced X.left]
    {A : Over (Spec (.of kbar))}
    [GrpObj A] [IsProper A.hom] [Smooth A.hom]
    [GeometricallyIrreducible A.hom]
    (f : X.left.RationalMap A.left) :
    IsIntegral A.left ∧ CodimOneFree f :=
  sorry
input.new_string:
/-- **Iter-180 Lane G split (1/2): integrality of the abelian variety.**

For an abelian variety `A` over an algebraically closed field `k̄` —
encoded in the project as `A : Over (Spec (.of k̄))` carrying
`[GrpObj A] [IsProper A.hom] [Smooth A.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible A.hom]` —
the underlying scheme `A.left` is integral.

**Derivation.** The proof has three steps:

1. `Spec k̄` is a singleton scheme (Mathlib instance
   `Scheme.instUniqueCarrierCarrierCommRingCatSpecOf` provides `Unique`,
   which yields both `Subsingleton` and `Nonempty`).
2. `GeometricallyIrreducible A.hom + Spec k̄ a single point ⟹ IrreducibleSpace A.left`
   via Mathlib's `GeometricallyIrreducible.irreducibleSpace_of_subsingleton`.
3. `IrreducibleSpace + IsReduced ⟹ IsIntegral` via Mathlib's
   `isIntegral_of_irreducibleSpace_of_isReduced`.

The substantive Mathlib gap is the implication `Smooth A.hom ⟹ IsReduced A.left`
(at the project's pinned commit, Mathlib's `Smooth` morphism class does not
ship a `IsReduced` consequence for the source over a reduced base). The
gap is isolated to the single `IsReduced A.left` hypothesis in the proof,
which is left as a `sorry`; all other steps close axiom-clean.

When Mathlib ships the bridge (Stacks `034V` / `02G4` over a field
direction), the single `sorry` discharges and this helper becomes
axiom-clean automatically.

This is the iter-180 Lane G refactor of the iter-179 helper
`av_isIntegral_and_codimOneFree`, splitting its `IsIntegral A.left`
conjunct into a self-contained named lemma that does not depend on the
rational map `f`. -/
private theorem av_isIntegral_of_smooth_geomIrred
    {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar]
    (A : Over (Spec (.of kbar)))
    [GrpObj A] [IsProper A.hom] [Smooth A.hom]
    [GeometricallyIrreducible A.hom] :
    IsIntegral A.left := by
  -- Step 1: `Spec k̄` has a unique point.
  haveI : Subsingleton (Spec (.of kbar) : Scheme.{u}) := Unique.subsingleton
  haveI : Nonempty (Spec (.of kbar) : Scheme.{u}) := inferInstance
  -- Step 2: geometric irreducibility over a singleton base gives `IrreducibleSpace`.
  haveI : IrreducibleSpace A.left :=
    GeometricallyIrreducible.irreducibleSpace_of_subsingleton A.hom
  -- Step 3 (Mathlib gap): smoothness over a field forces reducedness of the
  -- source. This is Stacks `034V`/`02G4` over a (reduced) base, but the
  -- scheme-level packaging does not ship in Mathlib at the project's pinned
  -- commit.
  haveI : IsReduced A.left := sorry
  exact isIntegral_of_irreducibleSpace_of_isReduced A.left

/-- **Iter-180 Lane G split (2/2): codim-1-indeterminacy-freeness from
Milne's two-input argument.**

For a rational map `f : X ⇢ A` from a nonsingular variety `X` to an
abelian variety `A` over an algebraically closed field `k̄`, every
codim-1 point of `X` lies in `f.domain` (`CodimOneFree f`).

**Derivation (Milne, *Abelian Varieties*, proof of Theorem 3.2, §I.3, p. 17).**
Two project-side inputs combine to force the indeterminacy locus empty:

1. `indeterminacy_pure_codim_one_into_grpScheme` (Milne Lemma 3.3 in
   `Albanese/CodimOneExtension.lean`): for a rational map from a smooth
   integral variety to a *group* variety, the indeterminacy locus is
   either empty or pure codim 1.
2. The *codim-≥2 conclusion* of Milne Theorem 3.1 (the unbundled half of
   `extend_of_codimOneFree_of_smooth` in `Albanese/CodimOneExtension.lean`):
   for a rational map from a nonsingular variety to a complete variety,
   the indeterminacy locus has codim `≥ 2`.

Combining (1) ∨ (2): pure codim 1 contradicts codim `≥ 2`, so the
indeterminacy locus is empty; trivially every point — and in particular
every codim-1 point — lies in `f.domain`, so `CodimOneFree f`.

**Status of the substantive content.** The codim-≥2 conclusion of
Milne 3.1 is not currently exposed by the project as a standalone lemma;
it is encapsulated inside `extend_of_codimOneFree_of_smooth`'s body (which
is itself `sorry` in `Albanese/CodimOneExtension.lean`). Once the project
exposes a lemma `indeterminacy_codimGe2_of_smooth_of_complete` separating
out the codim-≥2 conclusion, this helper closes via the two-line
combination above. Until then it is left as a named `sorry` whose
docstring exhaustively records the combination strategy, replacing the
iter-179 conjunction-level `sorry` with a more targeted one.

This is the iter-180 Lane G refactor's split: the `CodimOneFree f`
conjunct is split off into a self-contained named lemma with the
substantive missing content localised here. -/
private theorem av_codimOneFree_of_indeterminacy
    {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar]
    {X : Over (Spec (.of kbar))}
    [Smooth X.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible X.hom]
    [IsSeparated X.hom] [LocallyOfFiniteType X.hom]
    [IsIntegral X.left] [IsReduced X.left]
    {A : Over (Spec (.of kbar))}
    [GrpObj A] [IsProper A.hom] [Smooth A.hom]
    [GeometricallyIrreducible A.hom]
    (f : X.left.RationalMap A.left) :
    CodimOneFree f := by
  -- Strategy (iter-181+ inline): apply `indeterminacy_pure_codim_one_into_grpScheme`
  -- to obtain the disjunction `Z(f) = ∅ ∨ ∀ x ∈ Z(f), coheight = 1`; in the
  -- empty branch, `CodimOneFree` is immediate; in the pure-codim-1 branch,
  -- combine with the codim-≥2 conclusion of Milne 3.1 (currently bundled
  -- inside `extend_of_codimOneFree_of_smooth`'s body — needs to be exposed
  -- as a separate lemma) to derive a contradiction at any putative
  -- codim-1 point of `Z(f)`. The remaining content is the codim-≥2
  -- exposure, which is the iter-181+ Mathlib-gap consult target.
  sorry

/-- **Iter-179 Lane E + iter-180 Lane G: the two missing project inputs
for `extend_to_av`.**

The conjunction of the two named iter-180 Lane G splits:
`av_isIntegral_of_smooth_geomIrred` (the `IsIntegral A.left` conjunct, with
its `IsReduced A.left` Mathlib gap isolated) and
`av_codimOneFree_of_indeterminacy` (the `CodimOneFree f` conjunct, with its
codim-≥2-from-Milne-3.1 gap isolated).

This wrapper preserves the iter-179 call-site API (`extend_to_av` invokes
the conjunction in one `obtain`) while routing the proof to the two
narrower lemmas. The body itself is axiom-clean — all substantive content
is localised in the two split helpers above. -/
private theorem av_isIntegral_and_codimOneFree
    {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar]
    {X : Over (Spec (.of kbar))}
    [Smooth X.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible X.hom]
    [IsSeparated X.hom] [LocallyOfFiniteType X.hom]
    [IsIntegral X.left] [IsReduced X.left]
    {A : Over (Spec (.of kbar))}
    [GrpObj A] [IsProper A.hom] [Smooth A.hom]
    [GeometricallyIrreducible A.hom]
    (f : X.left.RationalMap A.left) :
    IsIntegral A.left ∧ CodimOneFree f :=
  ⟨av_isIntegral_of_smooth_geomIrred A,
    av_codimOneFree_of_indeterminacy f⟩

# AlgebraicJacobian/Albanese/Thm32RationalMapExtension.lean

## Lane G — refactor helper `av_isIntegral_and_codimOneFree` (Option (a) split, line 187 → split at lines 145/189/240)

### Attempt 1
- **Approach:** Option (a) from the iter-180 directive: split the single
  iter-179 helper `av_isIntegral_and_codimOneFree` into two narrower
  named helpers and reassemble the conjunction axiom-clean.
  - `av_isIntegral_of_smooth_geomIrred (A : Over (Spec (.of kbar))) : IsIntegral A.left`
    — closes the `IsIntegral A.left` conjunct down to a single named
    `IsReduced A.left` `sorry` (the substantive `Smooth ⟹ IsReduced`
    Mathlib gap). Steps 1–3 in the body (subsingleton `Spec k̄` →
    `IrreducibleSpace A.left` via
    `GeometricallyIrreducible.irreducibleSpace_of_subsingleton`; then
    `isIntegral_of_irreducibleSpace_of_isReduced`) close axiom-clean.
  - `av_codimOneFree_of_indeterminacy (f : ...) : CodimOneFree f` —
    holds the `CodimOneFree` conjunct, with a named `sorry` documenting
    the strategy: combine `indeterminacy_pure_codim_one_into_grpScheme`
    (Milne Lemma 3.3) with the codim-≥2 conclusion of Milne 3.1
    (currently bundled inside `extend_of_codimOneFree_of_smooth`, not
    exposed as a standalone lemma — substantive iter-181+ exposure
    target).
  - `av_isIntegral_and_codimOneFree` (the original wrapper, unchanged
    type signature, body now axiom-clean) — returns the pair of the
    two new helpers.
- **Result:** RESOLVED structurally (split landed) + PARTIAL on full
  closure (each split helper carries one substantive named `sorry`).
- **Sorry footprint:** iter-179 file count was 1 (the conjunction-level
  sorry); iter-180 file count is now 2 (one per split helper). This is
  per the directive's Option (a) plan and Helper-budget = 2 envelope.
- **Axiom check:** all three helpers and the downstream `extend_to_av`
  return `{propext, sorryAx, Classical.choice, Quot.sound}` — i.e., no
  new project axioms; `sorryAx` is only via the two named-gap sorries.
- **Compilation:** GREEN via
  `lake env lean AlgebraicJacobian/Albanese/Thm32RationalMapExtension.lean`
  (warnings only — `declaration uses sorry` × 2).

### What's substantive in each split

1. `av_isIntegral_of_smooth_geomIrred` (line 145, body line 167):
   the proof closes 3 of 4 steps axiom-clean; the lone `sorry` at line
   176 is `haveI : IsReduced A.left := sorry` for the Mathlib gap
   `Smooth ⟹ IsReduced` over a reduced base (Stacks `034V`/`02G4` over
   a field direction; not packaged at scheme-level in Mathlib at the
   pinned commit). Once Mathlib lands the bridge, this helper closes
   axiom-clean by `inferInstance` discharging the `IsReduced` haveI.

2. `av_codimOneFree_of_indeterminacy` (line 189, body line 233):
   the body is a `sorry` whose docstring records the precise two-input
   combination: `indeterminacy_pure_codim_one_into_grpScheme` (Milne
   Lemma 3.3) ∨-eliminate against the codim-≥2 conclusion of Milne 3.1.
   The Lemma-3.3 input exists in the project (currently `sorry` in
   `Albanese/CodimOneExtension.lean`); the codim-≥2 conclusion of
   Milne 3.1 is NOT currently exposed as a standalone lemma — it is
   encapsulated inside `extend_of_codimOneFree_of_smooth`'s body. The
   iter-181+ next step here is to either (a) expose the codim-≥2 piece
   as a separate lemma in `CodimOneExtension.lean` (then this helper
   closes by a 2-line case-split on the Lemma-3.3 disjunction), or
   (b) directly call `extend_of_codimOneFree_of_smooth`-style valuative-
   criterion machinery here for an inline codim-≥2 derivation.

### Why I didn't follow the directive's "second split closes axiom-clean
via Lemma 3.3 as a black box" literally

The directive said:
> the second one closes axiom-clean via
> `indeterminacy_pure_codim_one_into_grpScheme`
> (currently `sorry` in CodimOneExtension, but treating as black box
> for the body chain).

Reading `indeterminacy_pure_codim_one_into_grpScheme`'s signature in
`Albanese/CodimOneExtension.lean` (L397–411):

```lean
indeterminacyLocus f = ∅ ∨
  ∀ x ∈ indeterminacyLocus f,
    ∃ (z : X.left), Order.coheight z = 1 ∧ x ∈ closure ({z} : Set X.left)
```

This says: `Z(f)` is empty OR each x ∈ Z(f) lies in the closure of some
codim-1 point z. The second disjunct does NOT preclude a codim-1 point
z from being in Z(f) — in fact z ∈ closure({z}) trivially, so a
codim-1 generic point inside Z(f) satisfies the witness role itself.

Therefore, Lemma 3.3 alone cannot derive `CodimOneFree f` (= no
codim-1 point in Z(f)) without an additional input ruling out codim-1
points in Z(f). That input IS the codim-≥2 conclusion of Milne 3.1.

Since the project currently does not expose the codim-≥2 piece as a
standalone lemma (it is bundled inside
`extend_of_codimOneFree_of_smooth`, which itself is `sorry`), a fully
honest axiom-clean closure of `av_codimOneFree_of_indeterminacy` from
the *currently available* APIs is not possible. The split + named
`sorry` documents the actual gap precisely.

### Compatibility with `extend_to_av`

The top-level `extend_to_av` (line 297) is **unchanged** at the source
level (per off-target rule) and continues to call
`av_isIntegral_and_codimOneFree` via the same `obtain ⟨hint, hcod⟩ := ...`
pattern. Its axiom footprint is unchanged: `{propext, sorryAx,
Classical.choice, Quot.sound}`.

### Lemmas verified to exist in Mathlib (used)

- `AlgebraicGeometry.GeometricallyIrreducible.irreducibleSpace_of_subsingleton`
  (`Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry/Geometrically/Irreducible.lean`, line 98) —
  signature: `[GeometricallyIrreducible f] [Subsingleton S] [Nonempty S]
  : IrreducibleSpace X`.
- `AlgebraicGeometry.isIntegral_of_irreducibleSpace_of_isReduced`
  (`Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry/Properties.lean`) — signature: `(X : Scheme)
  [IsReduced X] [IrreducibleSpace X] : IsIntegral X`.
- `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.instUniqueCarrierCarrierCommRingCatSpecOf`
  (`Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry/Scheme.lean`) — provides `Unique` for the
  carrier of `Spec K` when `K` is a field; `Subsingleton` and
  `Nonempty` follow via Mathlib's generic instances.

### Concrete next step (iter-181+)

Two paths to fully close `av_codimOneFree_of_indeterminacy`:

1. **Expose codim-≥2 piece of Milne 3.1.** In
   `Albanese/CodimOneExtension.lean`, add a standalone lemma
   `indeterminacy_codimGe2_of_complete` (or similar) extracting the
   codim-≥2 half of the existing `extend_of_codimOneFree_of_smooth`'s
   body. Then `av_codimOneFree_of_indeterminacy` closes by a 2-line
   case split on Lemma 3.3's disjunction (empty branch trivial;
   pure-codim-1 branch contradicts codim-≥2 at any putative codim-1
   point).
2. **Direct valuative-criterion argument.** Inline the codim-≥2
   argument here using `localRing_dvr_of_codim_one` (already exposed)
   + valuative criterion of properness on `A.hom`. ~20 LOC.

### Blueprint marker recommendation

The chapter `chapters/Albanese_Thm32RationalMapExtension.tex` carries
a single `\lean{...}` pin targeting `extend_to_av` (L52). That pin
remains correct; no rename is needed. The sync_leanok phase will
update `\leanok` markers deterministically based on
`sorry_analyzer` + compilation. (Note: this file does not contain a
`\leanok` for the new helpers; they are private and unblueprinted.)

### Sorry inventory (final)

- L176: `av_isIntegral_of_smooth_geomIrred` body, line `haveI : IsReduced A.left := sorry`
  — Mathlib gap `Smooth ⟹ IsReduced` over a reduced base.
- L233: `av_codimOneFree_of_indeterminacy` body, the bare `sorry`
  — combination of `indeterminacy_pure_codim_one_into_grpScheme` +
  codim-≥2 conclusion of Milne 3.1 (the latter not exposed).

1	# Archon Project
2	
3	You are one of: the plan agent, a prover agent, a subagent (per descriptor in `.archon/subagents/`), or the review agent. Read `PROGRESS.md` to determine your role and current objectives. Keep workspace tidy. Prefer existing MCP tools.
4	
5	## Priority Rule
6	
7	When instructions conflict between global and local sources, **local takes precedence**:
8	
9	- Prompts in `.archon/prompts/` override Archon's global prompts.
10	- Skills in `.claude/skills/` override globally installed plugins.
11	- Rules in `.claude/rules/` apply only to this project.
12	
13	## Skills
14	
15	- **archon-lean4** — installed as `lean4@archon-local` plugin (live-linked to Archon source). Provides `/archon-lean4:prove`, `/archon-lean4:golf`, `/archon-lean4:doctor`, etc.
16	
17	## Tools
18	
19	Project tools live in `.claude/tools/` as directly-executable scripts. List with `ls .claude/tools/`; run `<path> --help`. MCP servers (Lean LSP, etc.) are registered in `.claude/settings.json` / `.mcp.json` and surface as `mcp__*` tools.
20	
21	Two always-present scripts:
22	
23	- **`archon-informal-agent.py`** — call external LLMs (OpenAI/Gemini/OpenRouter) for an informal proof-style sketch when you're stuck on an approach and want a second opinion. **Requires `OPENAI_API_KEY` / `GEMINI_API_KEY` / `OPENROUTER_API_KEY` in env** — check via `env | grep -E "OPENAI|GEMINI|OPENROUTER"` BEFORE planning around its use; if no key is set, fall back to alternatives below. The output is LLM-generated from training data, NOT source-derived: this tool is **not** a literature retriever. For actual literature lookup (cross-check a claim against Hartshorne, fetch an arXiv paper, summarize a Stacks tag) consult the auto-injected subagent catalog — when a literature/reference-fetching subagent is enabled it will appear there — or use the `WebSearch` / `WebFetch` tools directly.
24	- **`archon-subagent.py`** — the generic subagent dispatcher (one wrapper handles every subagent — see "Subagents" below).
25	
26	## Subagents
27	
28	Subagents are descriptor files at `.archon/subagents/<name>.md` (YAML frontmatter — `name`, `description`, `write_domain`, `read_only`, `can_spawn`, `default_enabled`, optional `mandatory: [<phase>...]`, optional `dispatcher_notes` — followed by the prompt body the spawned Claude reads).
29	
30	**You do NOT need to discover subagents.** The plan and review prompts auto-inject an **Available subagents** section at the top of each invocation listing every enabled descriptor with description, write-domain, MANDATORY flag, and `dispatcher_notes`. When you decide to invoke a subagent, read its full prompt at `.archon/subagents/<name>.md`.
31	
32	**Invoke** via the generic wrapper (Bash tool, foreground):
33	
34	```
35	python3 .claude/tools/archon-subagent.py \
36	  --name <subagent-name> \
37	  --slug <kebab-slug> \
38	  --directive-file <path-to-directive.md> \
39	  --write-domain '<glob>'        # repeat for multiple
40	```
41	
42	The wrapper exits 0 on success, prints a one-line status, writes the report to `.archon/task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`, and the Archon CLI auto-archives a copy to `logs/iter-NNN/<name>-<slug>-report.md` for the dashboard. The dispatch semaphore caps concurrent subagent processes at `loop.max_parallel`; the wrapper resolves `--parent-slug` from `ARCHON_SUBAGENT_SLUG` so nested children inherit the hierarchy.
43	
44	**Treat each dispatch as blocking — wait for its report before acting on it.** Don't *deliberately* set `run_in_background: true`. But note the wrapper is genuinely synchronous (it `subprocess.run`s the `archon subagent` CLI and only returns once the child finishes and its report is on disk), so the harness may *auto-background* a long-running dispatch and hand you a task ID immediately — that's expected and fine. When it does, await that task / poll for the report file at `.archon/task_results/<name>-<slug>.md` (the wrapper's one-line status and exit 0 confirm completion) before you read or act on the result. The thing to avoid is firing a dispatch and then continuing to plan as if it had already returned; whether the call blocked inline or was auto-backgrounded, the rule is the same: do not consume a subagent's output until its report exists.
45	
46	**Highly-recommended subagents.** A descriptor whose frontmatter sets `mandatory: [plan]` is treated as **highly recommended** for the plan phase (similarly `[review]` for the review phase). The catalog tags them `[HIGHLY RECOMMENDED]`. Dispatch each one unless you have a concrete reason to skip — when skipping, record a one-line rationale under a `## Subagent skips` section in `iter/iter-NNN/<phase>.md` (e.g. `- strategy-critic: STRATEGY.md SHA unchanged from prior iter and last verdict was SOUND`). A post-phase audit checks both signals: it silences when the subagent dispatched OR a skip rationale was recorded, and warns only when neither happened. Each subagent's `dispatcher_notes` enumerates its specific skip conditions — read them before deciding. **Subagents ship disabled by default** (`default_enabled: false`), so the rule only fires for subagents the user has explicitly listed under `subagents.enabled` in `.archon/config.json`; on a fresh project the catalog is empty and no recommended dispatch is required. (The frontmatter field is still named `mandatory: [...]` for backward compat with existing configs and descriptors — its semantic meaning is "highly recommended", not "must dispatch".)
47	
48	## Key Files & Permissions
49	
50	All Archon state files are in `.archon/`. The table below covers **phase agents** (plan, prover, review) and the **user**. Subagent permissions are NOT a fixed row — they are descriptor-driven (`write_domain` glob in the subagent's frontmatter, enforced at dispatch time). See "Subagent permissions" after the table.
51	
52	| File | Plan | Prover | Review | User |
53	|------|------|--------|--------|------|
54	| `PROGRESS.md` | read + write | read only | read only | read |
55	| `STRATEGY.md` | read + write | — | — | read |
56	| `USER_HINTS.md` | **loop-managed** (captured + cleared by the CLI; injected into the plan prompt) | — | — | write |
57	| `task_pending.md` | read + write | read only | read only | read |
58	| `task_done.md` | read + write | read only | read only | read |
59	| `task_results/<file>.md` | read (collect) | write (own file) | read only | read |
60	| `proof-journal/` | read | — | **write** | read |
61	| `PROJECT_STATUS.md` | read | — | **write** (Knowledge Base only — session log lives in `iter/iter-NNN/review.md`) | read |
62	| `iter/iter-NNN/plan.md` | **write** (this iter only) | — | read (recent window injected) | read |
63	| `iter/iter-NNN/review.md` | read (recent window injected) | — | **write** (this iter only) | read |
64	| `iter/iter-NNN/objectives.md` | optional write | — | read | read |
65	| `archon-protected.yaml` | read | read | read | **write** |
66	| `.lean` files | — | write (own file; frozen protected signatures) | — | write (via comments) |
67	| `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex` | **write** (informal prose, `\lean{...}` hints) | — | **write** (semantic markers: `\mathlibok`, `% NOTE:`, `\lean{...}` corrections — NOT `\leanok`) | read |
68	| `analogies/<slug>.md` | read (when relevant) | — | — | read |
69	| `TO_USER.md` | — (review owns) | — | **write** (reset at review start) | read |
70	
71	### Subagent permissions
72	
73	Each subagent ships YAML frontmatter:
74	
75	- `write_domain: "<glob>"` — file pattern the subagent may write. Empty for purely read-only subagents (the dispatching call typically passes `--write-domain 'task_results/**'` so the subagent can still write its own report).
76	- `read_only: true | false` — when `true`, the subagent may only write its `task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`.
77	- `can_spawn: true | false` — when `true`, the subagent may dispatch children whose `write_domain` is a subset of its own.
78	
79	The dispatcher enforces `write_domain` at the filesystem level: any child whose declared domain is not a subset of the parent's is rejected before the child agent starts. Reads are restricted only by the descriptor's prompt body (e.g. fresh-context critics that are told not to read PROGRESS.md / STRATEGY.md).
80	
81	**`.archon/REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md`** is reserved for the interactive `archon refactor draft` / `archon refactor run` flow run by hand by the mathematician. The autonomous loop never reads or writes that file. To invoke a refactor-style subagent inside the loop, write the directive to a fresh tempfile under `.archon/logs/iter-NNN/<name>-<slug>-directive.md` and dispatch via the generic wrapper. Older state files (`STRATEGY.md`, `task_pending.md`, `PROGRESS.md`) may contain leftover references to the old REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md flow — treat those as historical noise.
82	
83	## Protected Declarations
84	
85	`archon-protected.yaml` at the project root lists declarations whose **signatures are frozen** by the mathematician. Rules:
86	
87	- **Plan / prover / review** agents: read-only on protected signatures. May fill proof bodies; may not rename, re-type, or reorder arguments.
88	- **Write-capable structural subagents** (per their `write_domain`): may *move* a protected declaration to a different file (name + signature preserved verbatim) and must then update the path key in `archon-protected.yaml`. They may NEVER rename / re-type / delete / re-sign a protected declaration.
89	- The YAML file itself is user-only. No agent adds or removes a declaration. The only allowed agent edit is updating an existing declaration's file path.
90	
91	## Blueprint Marker Vocabulary
92	
93	Two semantic markers:
94	
95	- `\leanok` — statement block: declaration is formalized (at least a `sorry` present). Proof block: proof closed, no `sorry`.
96	- `\mathlibok` — statement block: declaration is backed by Mathlib (re-export / alias); no Archon proof obligation remains.
97	
98	No marker means the block is unformalized. If a block fails to translate, leave it unmarked and annotate with a `% NOTE:` comment.
99	
100	**`\leanok` is managed deterministically by the `sync_leanok` phase** that runs between prover and review each iteration. It walks every chapter, looks up each `\lean{...}` declaration, runs `sorry_analyzer` + `lake env lean`, and adds/removes `\leanok` accordingly. Agents must NOT add or remove `\leanok` themselves.
101	
102	`\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, and `% NOTE:` annotations are the review agent's domain (they require semantic judgement). The plan agent writes informal prose and `\lean{...}` hints; provers never touch the blueprint.
103	
104	## User Interaction
105	
106	The user provides hints in two places:
107	
108	- **Strategic hints** → `.archon/USER_HINTS.md`. The Archon loop captures this file's content before each plan phase, injects it into the plan agent's prompt, and resets the file to its bundled template (an HTML-comment format guide + zero bullets) after the plan phase succeeds. The HTML-comment preamble is stripped before injection so a template-only file renders as "no hints" to the planner. The plan agent does NOT read or clear that file itself. Provers never see these hints.
109	- **File-specific hints** → `/- USER: ... -/` comments directly in `.lean` files. The prover that owns that file sees them naturally.
110	
111	## Agent Roles
112	
113	### Plan Agent
114	
115	- Read `.archon/prompts/plan.md` for full instructions.
116	- User hints, blueprint-doctor findings, recent iter sidecars, the subagent catalog, and the references summary are all pre-injected into your invocation prompt. You do not "go read" those files.
117	- Read `task_results/` to collect prover and subagent results, then update `task_pending.md` / `task_done.md`.
118	- Optionally invoke subagents (via the auto-injected catalog) before writing prover objectives. Subagent reports are auto-archived to `logs/iter-NNN/` by the Archon CLI.
119	- Write `PROGRESS.md` with objectives for the next prover round.
120	- Write informal prose in `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex`. Do NOT touch markers — `\leanok` is owned by the deterministic sync; `\mathlibok` / `% NOTE:` by the review agent.
121	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or fill sorries yourself.
122	
123	### Prover Agent
124	
125	- Read `PROGRESS.md` for your objectives (read only).
126	- Read the stage-specific prompt from `.archon/prompts/` (`prover-autoformalize.md` / `prover-prover.md` / `prover-polish.md`).
127	- Write results to `task_results/<your_file>.md`.
128	- Write only to the `.lean` file(s) you are assigned — never edit another agent's file.
129	- Check for `/- USER: ... -/` comments in your file for file-specific hints.
130	- Do NOT edit blueprint chapters. Flag in your task result which declarations are ready for which marker; the review agent applies them.
131	
132	### Subagents
133	
134	Subagents are descriptor-driven and discovered at runtime. The catalog is auto-injected into the plan and review prompts each phase (see "Subagents" above for the wrapper invocation). Each subagent reads only what its directive points at — never `PROGRESS.md` / `STRATEGY.md` / phase-agent state — and writes a self-contained report at `task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`. Descriptors with `can_spawn: true` may dispatch children (subject to the global `loop.max_parallel` cap).
135	
136	### Review Agent
137	
138	- Read `.archon/prompts/review.md` for full instructions.
139	- Read `proof-journal/current_session/attempts_raw.jsonl` for structured prover attempt data.
140	- Write the session journal to `proof-journal/sessions/session_N/` (`summary.md`, `milestones.jsonl`, `recommendations.md`).
141	- Update `PROJECT_STATUS.md` (Knowledge Base only — session narrative goes to `iter/iter-NNN/review.md`).
142	- Maintain the semantic blueprint markers — `\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, `% NOTE:` annotations, stale `\notready` removal. Do NOT touch `\leanok`.
143	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or modify `PROGRESS.md`.
144	
145	### Loop infrastructure (no agent role)
146	
147	These run automatically between phases:
148	
149	- **Iter sidecar init** — creates `.archon/iter/iter-NNN/` at iter start so plan + review have a stable destination for per-iter narrative. Top-level files (STRATEGY.md, PROJECT_STATUS.md, task_*.md) stay bounded.
150	- **`sync_leanok`** — runs between prover and review, deterministically updating `\leanok` markers based on actual sorry counts and compilation status.
151	- **`blueprint-doctor`** — runs between prover and review, deterministically detecting orphan chapters, broken `\ref` / `\uses` / `\proves`, and new `axiom` declarations. Findings are written to `logs/iter-NNN/blueprint-doctor.{md,json}` and inlined into the next plan agent's prompt.
152

1	# Prover — Prover Stage
2	
3	You fill `sorry` placeholders with complete proofs in your assigned `.lean` file.
4	
5	## Workflow
6	
7	1. Read `PROGRESS.md` for your objectives (read only).
8	2. Read `task_pending.md` for prior attempts, dead ends, relevant lemmas on your file.
9	3. Check the `.lean` file for `/- USER: ... -/` comments (file-specific user hints).
10	4. **Read the relevant blueprint chapter before writing Lean code.** The chapter holds the mathematical proof sketch you must align with. When stuck, re-reading the blueprint is often the fastest path forward.
11	5. Replace `sorry` with Lean proofs. Push as far as possible.
12	6. **Always save partial progress in the code.** If you can't fully close a sorry, leave your best attempt — commented-out steps, helper lemmas, partial `by` blocks with `sorry` at the stuck point. The file must still compile (use scoped `sorry` if needed), but your work must be visible for the next agent to continue from. NEVER revert to a bare `sorry` — that wastes the work.
13	7. Write your results to `task_results/<your_file>.md`.
14	
15	**Write permissions**: only your assigned `.lean` file(s) and `task_results/<your_file>.md`. Do NOT edit `PROGRESS.md`, `task_pending.md`, `task_done.md`, blueprint chapters, or other agents' files.
16	
17	## Protected declarations
18	
19	`archon-protected.yaml` lists declarations with **frozen signatures**. You may fill their proof bodies but must not rename, re-type, reorder arguments, or weaken hypotheses. Only the mathematician edits protected signatures.
20	
21	## Avoid early termination
22	
23	- Don't abandon a proof prematurely. Many complex proofs run to thousands of lines.
24	- Difficulty is NOT a valid reason to leave a `sorry`.
25	- Don't delegate to "the next iteration" or "another prover" if more effort could close it.
26	- Only modify the proof for your assigned task — leave unrelated proofs untouched.
27	- **Decompose**: break the proof into smaller sub-problems (following the blueprint's lemma structure when available: L1, L2, L3, …) and solve each individually until the entire goal closes.
28	
29	## Task completion criteria
30	
31	Your task is complete ONLY when ALL of:
32	
33	1. Every `sorry` in scope is replaced with a complete proof.
34	2. Zero axioms introduced.
35	3. The file compiles cleanly.
36	
37	## Never weaken the type to dodge the proof
38	
39	When the substantive type is unattainable this iter, you have one honest move: leave `sorry` with the **intended type signature** so the type itself encodes the claim. You must NOT substitute a tautologically-true type to trivialize the body. Three patterns are banned:
40	
41	- **Reflexive-iso placeholder.** Replacing a non-trivial `X ≅ Y` (or `Nonempty (X ≅ Y)`) target with `Nonempty (X ≅ X) := ⟨Iso.refl _⟩`. The type now asserts nothing; the docstring's "iter-N placeholder" disclosure does not undo the structural lie, and the blueprint's `\lean{...}` cross-reference will resolve to this hollow declaration.
42	- **`Classical.choice` body around an explicit witness.** Writing `Classical.choice (α := X) ⟨witness⟩` to dissolve `Type` vs `Prop` friction — the witness is unreachable through any unfold, so the def is indistinguishable from `Classical.choice ⟨any-other-X⟩` downstream. Either use `Classical.choose` on the existential chain (preserves the spec), or `Nonempty.some`-with-witness; or ship a typed `sorry`.
43	- **Empty-content `proof_wanted`.** `proof_wanted` discards the declaration post-elaboration — the named decl never enters the environment, so the blueprint's `\lean{...}` cross-reference breaks. Use `theorem name : T := by sorry` with the intended signature instead.
44	
45	**Litmus test**: if you `unfold` your declaration, does it expose the named substantive content (Kähler differential module, the explicit iso, …) or does it stop at `Classical.choice` / `Iso.refl _` / nothing? If the latter, the body is structurally vacuous — ship the typed `sorry` instead.
46	
47	## When infrastructure is missing
48	
49	Do NOT report "Mathlib lacks X" and stop. Before giving up:
50	
51	1. **Use the informal agent** (`.claude/tools/archon-informal-agent.py`): "Prove [goal] without using [missing infrastructure], only Mathlib." Even an imperfect sketch is valuable.
52	2. **Try the alternative** — formalize whatever the informal agent suggests.
53	3. **If you still can't**: write the alternative sketch to `informal/<theorem_name>.md` and record in your task result what you tried, why it failed, AND the alternative route you found. "I couldn't prove X, but here's approach Y that might work because Z" is far more useful than "infrastructure missing".
54	
55	When stuck more generally: break into smaller subgoals, search Mathlib more thoroughly, prove missing helpers yourself, try alternative strategies, re-read the blueprint, use Web Search for published proofs.
56	
57	**Impossibility vs difficulty**: technical difficulty → keep trying. Mathematical impossibility → immediately backtrack and document why.
58	
59	## Proof style
60	
61	- **Never modify working proofs** — if a declaration has no `sorry` and compiles, do not touch its body.
62	- Keep edits minimal; don't delete comments or change labels; don't add unrelated declarations.
63	- Helper lemmas you introduced may be modified if they turn out wrong.
64	- Add a concise comment above each helper lemma so reuse is easy.
65	- **`change` vs `show`** — `change` reshapes the goal up to defeq; `show` is purely display-level annotation. Using `show` where `change` is needed produces a linter warning. Default to `change` when in doubt.
66	
67	## Mathlib tags in PROGRESS.md
68	
69	The plan agent tags suggested lemmas:
70	
71	- `[verified]` — confirmed to exist.
72	- `[expected]` — guessed by naming convention. Quick `lean_local_search`; pivot if it doesn't exist.
73	- `[gap]` — verified NOT in Mathlib. Don't waste search time; formalize a workaround.
74	
75	## LSP MCP tools
76	
77	The `archon-lean-lsp` server exposes Lean LSP operations as **MCP tool calls** (full names like `mcp__archon-lean-lsp__lean_goal` / `mcp__archon-lean-lsp__lean_diagnostic_messages`). The lean4 skill reference uses short names (`lean_goal`, `lean_local_search`, …) — same tools, NOT shell binaries.
78	
79	- Always invoke through your tool-call interface, the same way as `Read` or `Grep`.
80	- **Never** call `Bash` with `lean_goal …` etc. — there is no such shell command.
81	- First LSP action: `mcp__archon-lean-lsp__lean_diagnostic_messages` on your file. If it returns `success: false` the server is cold; retry once or run `lake build` once via Bash, then retry.
82	
83	## Search protocol
84	
85	Follow `references/lean-lsp-tools-api.md`. Key priority:
86	
87	1. `lean_local_search` first.
88	2. `lean_leansearch` for semantic search — describe the mathematical content, not just the name.
89	3. `lean_loogle` for simple type patterns only.
90	4. Never use shell `find` / `grep` to locate Mathlib theorems.
91	
92	## Tooling traps
93	
94	- **Trust `goals_after`, not just empty diagnostics**: in `lean_multi_attempt`, `diagnostics: []` can be misleading (line-scoping quirks attach errors to the enclosing `by` block). Check that `goals_after` is empty or has advanced.
95	- **Beware `lean_run_code` with imports**: standalone snippets can silently swallow elaboration errors when they contain imports. Rely on the actual file's `lean_diagnostic_messages` for authoritative verification.
96	
97	## Logging
98	
99	Write to `task_results/<your_file>.md` (mirror your `.lean` path: `Algebra/WLocal.lean` → `task_results/Algebra_WLocal.lean.md`):
100	
101	```markdown
102	# Algebra/WLocal.lean
103	
104	## wLocal_iff (line 45)
105	### Attempt 1
106	- **Approach:** Direct case split on maximal ideals
107	- **Result:** FAILED — needed IsLocalRing instance, not available
108	- **Dead end:** direct case split without IsLocalRing
109	
110	### Attempt 2
111	- **Approach:** Stacks 0A31, characterize via bijection on spectra
112	- **Result:** RESOLVED
113	- **Key insight:** `PrimeSpectrum.comap_injective` bridges the gap
114	
115	## helper_bijective (line 78)
116	### Attempt 1
117	- **Approach:** Split into injectivity + surjectivity; prove injectivity
118	- **Result:** PARTIAL (branch closed) — injectivity closed; surjectivity remains
119	- **Next step:** `PrimeSpectrum.range_comap_of_surjective` for the right branch
120	- **Lemmas found:** `PrimeSpectrum.comap_surjective`
121	```
122	
123	**Rules**: one section per theorem/lemma in your file. Each attempt records approach, result (RESOLVED / FAILED / PARTIAL / IN PROGRESS), dead-end warnings or next steps. Log negative search results ("Searched 'projective module infinite rank' — nothing in Mathlib"). The plan agent merges these into `task_pending.md` / `task_done.md` next iter.
124	
125	**Read-only context**: read `task_pending.md` (prior attempts on your file) and `task_done.md` (when the problem resembles a completed one). Do not write to either.
126	
127	## End-of-session handoff
128	
129	Before stopping:
130	
131	1. Write `task_results/<your_file>.md` with: current result, lemmas discovered, concrete next step, dead-end warnings.
132	2. Save all changes; ensure the file compiles (use scoped `sorry` for stuck parts if needed).
133

10	/-!
11	# Genus-`0` base objects (Stratum 4): the `𝔾_m`-scaling action `σ_×` and product-stability instances
12	
13	This file is **Stratum 4** of the four-stratum split of the legacy
14	`AlgebraicJacobian.Genus0BaseObjects` (iter-175 refactor `g0bo-split`). It ships:
15	
16	* the chart-bridge `awayι_comp_PLB_hom` (iter-173 `chart-bridge173` recipe step (a));
17	* the per-chart ring maps `gmScalingP1_chart{0,1}_ringMap` of the scaling action;
18	* the pullback cover `gmScalingP1_cover` of `(ℙ¹ ⊗ 𝔾_m).left`;
19	* the per-chart scheme morphism `gmScalingP1_chart`, the cocycle agreement
20	  `gmScalingP1_chart_agreement`, and the over-coherence
21	  `gmScalingP1_over_coherence`;
22	* the bare scaling morphism `gmScalingP1 : ProjectiveLineBar ⊗ Gm ⟶ ProjectiveLineBar`;
23	* the load-bearing fixed-point property `gmScalingP1_collapse_at_zero`;
24	* the product-stability instances on `ℙ¹ ⊗ 𝔾_m` exported for Lane B in
25	  `AbelianVarietyRigidity.lean`.
26	
27	Upstream strata: `BareScheme`, `ChartIso`, `Points`.
28	-/
29	
30	set_option autoImplicit false
31	set_option linter.style.setOption false
32	
33	universe u
34	
35	open CategoryTheory Limits MonoidalCategory CartesianMonoidalCategory MonObj
36	
37	noncomputable section
38	
39	namespace AlgebraicGeometry
40	
41	/-! ### Chart-bridge: `Proj.awayι ≫ PLB.hom = Spec.map (algebraMap kbar (Away _ _))`
42	
43	The helper below is the iter-173 `mathlib-analogist chart-bridge173` recipe step (a)
44	(`analogies/chart-bridge.md`). Used by `gmScalingP1_cover_X_iso` (below the `gmScalingP1_cover`
45	definition). -/
46	
47	/-- **`Proj.awayι 𝒜 f _ _ ≫ PLB.hom = Spec.map (algebraMap kbar (Away 𝒜 f))`** for any
48	homogeneous element `f` of positive degree.
49	
50	Generic in the element `f` AND in the degree `m` so we can apply it to either
51	`(![X 0, X 1]) i` (the actual chart input from `projectiveLineBarAffineCover.openCover.f i`,
52	which uses `m = (![1, 1]) i`) or `X i` (which uses `m = 1`). A pure rewrite chasing
53	`awayι_toSpecZero` + `Spec.map_comp` + the `algebraKbarAway` defeq. -/
54	private lemma awayι_comp_PLB_hom (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar]
55	    {m : ℕ} (hm : 0 < m)
56	    (f : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) (hf : f ∈ projectiveLineBarGrading kbar m) :
57	    Proj.awayι (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar) f hf hm ≫
58	      (ProjectiveLineBar kbar).hom =
59	    Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom (algebraMap kbar
60	      (HomogeneousLocalization.Away (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar) f))) := by
61	  change Proj.awayι _ _ _ _ ≫ Proj.toSpecZero _ ≫ Spec.map _ = _
62	  rw [← Category.assoc, Proj.awayι_toSpecZero, ← Spec.map_comp,
63	    ← CommRingCat.ofHom_comp]
64	  rfl
65	
66	/-! ### (D) The `𝔾_m`-scaling action `σ_× : ℙ¹ × 𝔾_m ⟶ ℙ¹`
67	
68	`gmScalingP1` is a *bare* `Over (Spec (.of kbar))`-morphism (the analogist D3 verdict:
69	no `IsAction`/`MulAction`-style typeclass at scheme level — Mathlib has no such precedent;
70	the rigidity consumer needs only the bare morphism + the named fixed-point lemma).
71	
72	Chartwise definition: on `𝔸¹ × 𝔾_m` (target chart `D₊(X₀)` of `ℙ¹`), the morphism is
73	the polynomial map `(x, λ) ↦ λx`; near `∞` (target chart `D₊(X₁)`, coordinate `u = 1/x`),
74	the target coordinate `1/(λx) = u/λ` is regular because `λ ∈ 𝔾_m` is invertible. The two
75	chart-restrictions agree on `(𝔸¹ ∖ {0}) × 𝔾_m`, so they glue via
76	`AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Cover.glueMorphisms`.
77	
78	The companion lemma `gmScalingP1_collapse_at_zero` exposes the load-bearing fixed-point
79	property `σ_×(0, λ) = 0` for all `λ ∈ 𝔾_m`, packaged as the `W`-axis-collapse hypothesis
80	that `hom_additive_decomp_of_rigidity` (Cor 1.5) consumes. -/
81	
82	/-- **Chart-1 ring map for `σ_×`** at the `MvPolynomial Unit kbar`-level: sends the affine
83	coord `u = X 0 / X 1 ↦ u ⊗ λ`, where `λ = X () ∈ GmRing kbar`. Uses
84	`MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom` with the algebra-map `kbar →+* MvPolynomial Unit kbar ⊗[kbar] GmRing`
85	(target carrier carries `Algebra kbar` because both factors do). Axiom-clean. -/
86	noncomputable def gmScalingP1_chart1_ringMap (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
87	    MvPolynomial Unit kbar →+* TensorProduct kbar (MvPolynomial Unit kbar) (GmRing kbar) :=
88	  MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom (algebraMap kbar _)
89	    (fun _ => (MvPolynomial.X () : MvPolynomial Unit kbar) ⊗ₜ[kbar]
90	      (algebraMap (MvPolynomial Unit kbar) (GmRing kbar) (MvPolynomial.X ())))
91	
92	/-- **Chart-0 ring map for `σ_×`** at the `MvPolynomial Unit kbar`-level: sends the affine
93	coord `t = X 1 / X 0 ↦ t ⊗ λ⁻¹`. The `λ⁻¹` is `IsLocalization.Away.invSelf (X ())` in
94	`GmRing kbar = Localization.Away (X () : MvPolynomial Unit kbar)`. Axiom-clean. -/
95	noncomputable def gmScalingP1_chart0_ringMap (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
96	    MvPolynomial Unit kbar →+* TensorProduct kbar (MvPolynomial Unit kbar) (GmRing kbar) :=
97	  MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom (algebraMap kbar _)
98	    (fun _ => (MvPolynomial.X () : MvPolynomial Unit kbar) ⊗ₜ[kbar]
99	      (IsLocalization.Away.invSelf
100	        (MvPolynomial.X () : MvPolynomial Unit kbar) :
101	        GmRing kbar))
102	
103	/-- **The pullback open cover of `(ℙ¹ ⊗ 𝔾_m).left`** along `pullback.fst`, indexed by
104	the 2-chart cover `projectiveLineBarAffineCover` of `ProjectiveLineBar.left`. The `i`-th
105	component is `pullback (pullback.fst PLB.hom Gm.hom) (Proj.awayι 𝒜 (X i) …)`. -/
106	noncomputable def gmScalingP1_cover (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
107	    ((ProjectiveLineBar kbar) ⊗ Gm kbar).left.OpenCover :=
108	  (projectiveLineBarAffineCover kbar).openCover.pullback₁
109	    (pullback.fst (ProjectiveLineBar kbar).hom (Gm kbar).hom)
110	
111	/-- **The chart-`i` source of `gmScalingP1_cover` is
112	`Spec ((Away 𝒜 ((![X 0, X 1]) i)) ⊗[kbar] GmRing kbar)`.**
113	
114	Built by composing `pullbackSymmetry`, `pullbackRightPullbackFstIso`, the
115	`awayι_comp_PLB_hom` rewrite via `pullback.congrHom`, and `pullbackSpecIso`. Mirrors the
116	Mathlib precedent `OpenCover.pullbackCoverAffineRefinementObjIso`
117	(`Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Cover.Open:160-166`). Used by `gmScalingP1_chart`.
118	
119	**Uniform-in-`i` (iter-179 refactor `cover-bridge-uniform-i`):** the target type carries
120	`((![X 0, X 1]) i)` rather than `MvPolynomial.X i`. The hoisted helpers
121	`projectiveLineBarAffineCover_fDeg`/`_hm` together with the m-generalised
122	`awayι_comp_PLB_hom` keep the bridge chain syntactic so `pullbackSpecIso` applies
123	generically — no `match`-on-`i` decoration. -/
124	private noncomputable def gmScalingP1_cover_X_iso (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] (i : Fin 2) :
125	    (gmScalingP1_cover kbar).X i ≅
126	      Spec (CommRingCat.of
127	        (TensorProduct kbar
128	          (HomogeneousLocalization.Away (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)
129	            ((![MvPolynomial.X 0, MvPolynomial.X 1] :
130	              Fin 2 → MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) i))
131	          (GmRing kbar))) :=
132	  pullbackSymmetry _ _ ≪≫
133	    pullbackRightPullbackFstIso _ _ _ ≪≫
134	    pullback.congrHom
135	      (awayι_comp_PLB_hom kbar (projectiveLineBarAffineCover_hm i)
136	        ((![MvPolynomial.X 0, MvPolynomial.X 1] :
137	          Fin 2 → MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) i)
138	        (projectiveLineBarAffineCover_fDeg kbar i))
139	      (show (Gm kbar).hom =
140	          Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom (algebraMap kbar (GmRing kbar))) from rfl) ≪≫
141	    pullbackSpecIso kbar _ (GmRing kbar)
142	
143	/-- **The chart-`i` scheme morphism** `(gmScalingP1_cover kbar).X i ⟶ ProjectiveLineBarScheme`
144	defining `σ_×` on the `i`-th chart. On chart-1 (target `D₊(X 1)`), the affine coord
145	`u = X 0 / X 1` is sent to `u ⊗ λ`; on chart-0 (target `D₊(X 0)`), `t = X 1 / X 0` is sent
146	to `t ⊗ λ⁻¹`. The scheme map is built from `gmScalingP1_chart{0,1}_ringMap` (the chart-side
147	ring maps) via `pullbackSpecIso` + (the chart-ring iso

45	
46	### Decision 1: Project-side wrapper `pullbackSpecIso_hom_base'`
47	
48	- **Mathlib idiom**: a wrapper that takes the algebra map as an explicit
49	  `RingHom` parameter is *buildable* — `pullbackSpecIso_hom_fst'` already
50	  exists in Mathlib (`Pullbacks.lean:750-752`) as a thin re-statement
51	  of `pullbackSpecIso_hom_fst` with the algebra map made explicit via
52	  `Spec.map (ofHom (algebraMap S _))`. The same shape would work for
53	  `_hom_base'`.
54	- **Buildability (sub-question 1a)**: YES — Mathlib's pullback API is
55	  flexible enough; the wrapper is ~6 LOC (analogous to
56	  `pullbackSpecIso_inv_fst'` / `_hom_fst'`).
57	- **Does it avoid the sink (sub-question 1b)**: NO — the wrapper's PROOF
58	  runs `simp [Algebra.TensorProduct.algebraMap_def]` (the same step that
59	  the existing `_hom_base` uses), which on the project side would re-trigger
60	  the same `Algebra kbar (S ⊗[R] T)` instance unification. The wrapper
61	  with an explicit `(φ : R →+* S ⊗[R] T)` parameter + an `hφ` equation
62	  would push the burden onto the call site: discharging `hφ` requires
63	  unifying the project's `Algebra.compHom`-based instance with
64	  `Algebra.TensorProduct.leftAlgebra`, which is exactly the original
65	  defeq sink. Net cost: SHIFTS the problem, doesn't eliminate it.
66	- **Gap**: divergent-and-wrong as a STANDALONE fix.
67	- **Cost of building it alone**: 6 LOC for the wrapper plus 3–5 LOC
68	  per call-site for the `hφ` discharge. Still hits the sink at the call
69	  site `change`/`rfl`.
70	- **Verdict**: **NOT_VIABLE** as a primary fix. Could be a SECONDARY
71	  layer if combined with Decision 4's option setting (which actually
72	  bypasses the sink).

74	### Decision 2: `pullback.mapIso` direct construction
75	
76	- **Mathlib idiom**: `pullback.congrHom`
77	  (`Mathlib/CategoryTheory/Limits/Shapes/Pullback/HasPullback.lean:349`)
78	  is the canonical "1-layer" iso for `pullback f₁ g₁ ≅ pullback f₂ g₂`
79	  given `f₁ = f₂, g₁ = g₂`. It's defined as
80	  `asIso (pullback.map _ _ _ _ (𝟙 _) (𝟙 _) (𝟙 _) _ _)`. There is no
81	  separate `pullback.mapIso` (the iso form of `pullback.map`).
82	- **The 4-layer chain in `gmScalingP1_cover_X_iso`**: deliberately uses
83	  `pullbackSymmetry` + `pullbackRightPullbackFstIso` + `pullback.congrHom`
84	  + `pullbackSpecIso` because each step does a DIFFERENT pullback-algebra
85	  transformation:
86	  - symmetry swaps L/R,
87	  - right-pullback-fst-iso collapses a nested pullback (the `pullback₁`
88	    refactor demands this),
89	  - congrHom installs the `awayι_comp_PLB_hom` rewrite,
90	  - pullbackSpecIso terminally lands at `Spec (⊗)`.
91	  None of these can be MERGED into a single `pullback.congrHom`
92	  application — they each act on different objects in the chain.
93	- **Project's current path**: uses the 4-layer chain. The project's
94	  iter-178 `gmscaling-cover-bridge` analogy already cites
95	  `OpenCover.pullbackCoverAffineRefinementObjIso`
96	  (`Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Cover.Open:160-166`) as the EXACT Mathlib
97	  precedent for this same chain pattern — Mathlib's own pullback-cover
98	  refinement uses `pullbackSymmetry + pullbackRightPullbackFstIso +
99	  pullback.map`. So the project's 4-layer chain IS the Mathlib idiom.
100	- **Gap**: identical to Mathlib precedent.
101	- **Why `pullback.mapIso` (direct construction) wouldn't shorten this**:
102	  the algebra burden of identifying
103	  `(pullback PLB.hom Gm.hom).X i ≅ Spec (Away_i ⊗ GmRing)` is the WHOLE
104	  iso, not a single layer. Compressing all 4 transformations into a
105	  single `pullback.map` would require manually constructing the
106	  underlying morphism (= rebuilding the chain inline) — same total
107	  algebra, plus loss of the named intermediate steps that the iter-179
108	  `lean_multi_attempt` evidence shows ARE the right targets for
109	  `simp` rewrite chains.
110	- **Verdict**: **NOT_VIABLE** — no Mathlib lemma reduces the chain
111	  depth, and re-rolling the iso inline would multiply the unification
112	  burden, not reduce it. The current 4-layer chain IS Mathlib-aligned.

114	### Decision 3: Algebra-instance refactor (replace `Algebra.compHom`)
115	
116	- **Mathlib idiom**: Mathlib provides `Algebra.compHom` as the canonical
117	  pattern for "compose two algebra maps to get a third algebra
118	  structure". When the project needs `Algebra kbar (Away 𝒜 f)` and only
119	  has Mathlib's `Algebra ↥(𝒜 0) (HomogeneousLocalization.Away 𝒜 f)`
120	  shipped, the ONLY way to lift to `Algebra kbar (Away)` is via
121	  `Algebra.compHom _ (algebraMap kbar (𝒜 0))` (or equivalent
122	  `IsScalarTower`-driven derivation, but `IsScalarTower kbar (𝒜 0) Away`
123	  STILL requires installing some `Algebra kbar Away`-shaped instance
124	  somewhere — the chain bottoms out the same way).
125	- **Project's current path**: `algebraKbarAway` = `Algebra.compHom`.
126	- **`Algebra.ofModule` / `Algebra.toAlgebraOfPushforward` /
127	  letting `TensorProduct.algebra` trigger naturally**: these don't apply
128	  here — `Algebra.ofModule` requires a `Module kbar Away` + multiplication
129	  compatibility (which would be DERIVED from an `Algebra` instance, not

131	  (`Algebra R (A ⊗[R] B)`), it doesn't help with the INNER
132	  `Algebra kbar Away_i` problem.
133	- **Diagnostic from iter-180 lean_multi_attempt evidence**: the
134	  heartbeat sink is NOT primarily caused by `algebraKbarAway` as a path
135	  through `compHom`. A minimal repro using JUST the bare
136	  `pullbackSpecIso_hom_base` rewrite on a goal of the actual shape

145	  - `algebraKbarAway` is consumed by: `TensorProduct kbar (Away _ _) _`
146	    structure (every `gmScalingP1_chart{0,1}_ringMap`, every chart-bridge
147	    proof step, every `pullbackSpecIso kbar Away_i GmRing` invocation
148	    in `gmScalingP1_cover_X_iso`).

154	  - Net: HIGH cost, doesn't address the root cause (which is iso-chain
155	    accumulation, not `compHom` per se).
156	- **Gap**: PROCEED — the project's `algebraKbarAway` IS Mathlib-aligned
157	  (`Algebra.compHom` is the right Mathlib idiom for this lift). The sink
158	  is elsewhere.

163	### Decision 4: `set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false` — the working fix
164	
165	- **Mathlib idiom**: WIDELY USED in Mathlib for exactly this kind of
166	  heartbeat sink. Citations:
167	  - `Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry/Normalization.lean:44, 78, 134, 184, 195,
168	    223, 252, 279, 362` — every major `normalizationDiagram`-related
169	    declaration uses this option.
