8	/-!
9	# The Quot scheme (A.2.b)
10	
11	This file is the **A.2.b** file-skeleton sub-build chapter for the project's
12	positive-genus arm of `nonempty_jacobianWitness`. It packages the
13	Grothendieck–Altman–Kleiman Quot-scheme construction
14	`Quot^{Φ,L}_{E/X/S}` — a projective `S`-scheme representing the functor of
15	`T`-flat coherent quotients of `E_T` on `X_T = X ×_S T` with Hilbert
16	polynomial `Φ` on every fiber — together with the in-project sub-build for
17	the Grassmannian *scheme* (Mathlib at the pinned commit carries only a
18	linear-algebra Grassmannian).
19	
20	## Status (iter-176 Lane H file-skeleton — re-dispatch)
21	
22	iter-175 Lane H died to the Anthropic session-limit reset window without
23	ever calling `Write` (the file was never created). iter-176 re-dispatches
24	the file-skeleton verbatim. Each of the six blueprint-pinned declarations
25	carries the *intended* substantive type signature (matching the
26	`\lean{...}` pin in `blueprint/src/chapters/Picard_QuotScheme.tex`) with a
27	`sorry` body. The bodies are iter-177+ work; the substantive proofs are
28	deep (Nitsure §5: boundedness ⟶ Grassmannian embedding ⟶ flattening
29	stratification ⟶ valuative criterion).
30	
31	The 6 blueprint-pinned declarations are:
32	
33	1. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.hilbertPolynomial` (def, ~5 LOC) — the
34	   **Hilbert polynomial function** `s ↦ Φ_{F,s} ∈ ℚ[λ]` of a coherent
35	   sheaf `F` on `X` over a finite-type `π : X ⟶ S` with respect to a
36	   line bundle `L`. Encoded as a function `S → Polynomial ℚ`.
37	
38	2. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.QuotFunctor` (def, ~6 LOC) — the **Quot
39	   functor** `Quot^{Φ,L}_{E/X/S} : (Sch/S)^op ⥤ Set` sending an
40	   `S`-scheme `T ⟶ S` to the set of equivalence classes
41	   `⟨F, q⟩` of pairs `(F, q)` with `F` a `T`-flat coherent sheaf on
42	   `X_T`, `q : E_T ↠ F` a surjection, and `F|_{X_t}` having Hilbert
43	   polynomial `Φ` at every `t ∈ T`.
44	
45	3. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Grassmannian` (def, ~5 LOC) — the
46	   **Grassmannian functor** `Grass(V, d) : (Sch/S)^op ⥤ Set` of
47	   rank-`d` quotients of a locally free `O_S`-module `V`.
48	
49	4. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Grassmannian.representable` (theorem, ~8 LOC)
50	   — the **representability of the Grassmannian** by a smooth projective
51	   `S`-scheme `Gr_S(V, d)` of relative dimension `d(r-d)`, equipped with
52	   the Plücker closed embedding into `ℙ_S(⋀^d V)`.
53	
54	5. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.QuotScheme` (theorem, ~10 LOC) — the
55	   **Grothendieck–Altman–Kleiman representability theorem** for the Quot
56	   functor: for a noetherian `S`, a projective `π : X ⟶ S`, a relatively
57	   very ample `L` on `X`, a coherent `E`, and `Φ ∈ ℚ[λ]`, the functor
58	   `Quot^{Φ,L}_{E/X/S}` is representable by a projective `S`-scheme.
59	
60	6. `AlgebraicGeometry.flatBaseChangeCohomology` (theorem, ~10 LOC) — the
61	   **flat base-change theorem of cohomology** (Stacks tag 02KH): for a
62	   cartesian square with `g` flat and `f` quasi-compact quasi-separated,
63	   the canonical base-change map `g* (f_* F) ⟶ f'_* ((g')* F)` is an
64	   isomorphism. The current scaffold encodes the `i = 0` direct-image
65	   form (substantive content of (ii) of the Stacks 02KH statement); the
66	   `R^i f_*` form for `i ≥ 1` requires the higher-direct-image
67	   infrastructure not present at the pinned commit.
68	
69	## Note on type expressivity
70	
71	Following the project rule "Never weaken the type to dodge the proof",
72	each declaration carries a substantive, non-tautological type:
73	
74	- `hilbertPolynomial` returns `Polynomial ℚ` keyed by `s : S`, not
75	  `Unit`; the Hilbert polynomial is a non-trivial invariant of the
76	  coherent sheaf at the fiber over `s`.
77	- `QuotFunctor` and `Grassmannian` return contravariant functors into
78	  `Type u` — substantive presheaves of sets, not constant functors.
79	- `Grassmannian.representable` and `QuotScheme` package the
80	  `Functor.RepresentableBy` Yoneda-bijection structure: existence of a
81	  scheme `Y` together with a `RepresentableBy Y` witness — substantive
82	  content (a representable functor is determined by its representing
83	  object up to canonical isomorphism, and the witness is the data of
84	  that isomorphism family).
85	- `flatBaseChangeCohomology` produces a `Nonempty (... ≅ ...)` of an
86	  isomorphism between two `S'`-modules built via the pullback/pushforward
87	  bifunctor; the iso is non-trivial (it is `Stacks 02KH` content, not
88	  the identity-on-the-same-object iso `Iso.refl _`).
89	
90	## Mathlib status
91	
92	Mathlib (master `b80f227`) provides:
93	- `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules` (the category `X.Modules`),
94	- `Scheme.Modules.pullback`, `Scheme.Modules.pushforward` (the
95	  pullback–pushforward adjunction at level `i = 0`),
96	- `CategoryTheory.IsPullback` for cartesian squares,
97	- `CategoryTheory.Functor.RepresentableBy` for representable functors,
98	- `AlgebraicGeometry.Flat`, `AlgebraicGeometry.QuasiCompact`,
99	  `AlgebraicGeometry.QuasiSeparated`, `AlgebraicGeometry.IsProper`,
100	  `AlgebraicGeometry.LocallyOfFiniteType`, `AlgebraicGeometry.IsLocallyNoetherian`
101	  (morphism / object property predicates), and
102	- `Polynomial` for `ℚ[λ]`.
103	
104	Mathlib does NOT provide (at the pinned commit):
105	- a Grassmannian *scheme* (only a linear-algebra Grassmannian
106	  as a finite-rank-quotient variety),
107	- a `IsProjective` morphism property,
108	- the Quot/Hilbert functor or its representability,
109	- `R^i f_*` higher direct images on `Scheme.Modules`,
110	- Castelnuovo–Mumford `m`-regularity,
111	- Snapper's Lemma for the polynomial property of Euler characteristics.
112	
113	The current file-skeleton uses `IsProper π` as the structural stand-in
114	for "projective `π`" (every projective morphism is proper; the
115	restriction is harmless in the Route A consumer setting where `π` comes
116	from a smooth proper curve, which is automatically projective).
117	iter-177+ refinement: once Mathlib gains an `IsProjective` morphism
118	property, the hypothesis tightens.
119	
120	## References
121	
122	Blueprint: `blueprint/src/chapters/Picard_QuotScheme.tex` (~900 LOC,
123	6 pins + 4 sub-lemmas). Source: Nitsure, "Construction of Hilbert and
124	Quot Schemes", §§1, 5 (FGA Explained Ch. 5, arXiv:math/0504020 pp. 5–35);
125	Grothendieck, FGA TDTE-IV; Stacks Project tag 02KH (flat-base-change of
126	cohomology).

139	/-! ## §1. Hilbert polynomial of a coherent sheaf
140	
141	For a finite-type morphism `π : X ⟶ S` with `S` noetherian and a coherent
142	sheaf `F` on `X` whose schematic support is proper over `S` (here encoded
143	as plain `X.Modules` for the file-skeleton), the per-fiber Hilbert
144	polynomial is the function
145	
146	`s ↦ Φ_{F,s} ∈ ℚ[λ],   Φ_{F,s}(m) = χ(X_s, F|_{X_s} ⊗ L_s^{⊗m})`.
147	
148	Snapper's Lemma ensures this is a polynomial in `m`; the proof requires
149	graded-Euler-characteristic infrastructure and is not stated here.
150	
151	Blueprint reference: `def:hilbert_polynomial` (Nitsure §1; cf. Hartshorne
152	III.5.2). -/
153	
154	/-- The **Hilbert polynomial** of a coherent sheaf `F` on `X` over `S` at
155	the fiber over `s ∈ S` with respect to a line bundle `L` on `X`.
156	
157	Encoded as a function `s ↦ Φ_{F,s} ∈ ℚ[λ]`. The defining formula
158	
159	`Φ_{F,s}(m) = χ(X_s, F|_{X_s} ⊗ L_s^{⊗ m})
160	            = Σ_i (-1)^i dim_{κ(s)} H^i(X_s, F|_{X_s} ⊗ L_s^{⊗m})`
161	
162	is a polynomial in `m` by Snapper's Lemma; the polynomial coefficients
163	depend on `s` through the fiber `F|_{X_s}`. When `F` is `S`-flat the
164	function `s ↦ Φ_{F,s}` is locally constant on `S`.
165	
166	iter-177+: the body unfolds to the graded-Euler-characteristic
167	construction once `χ` of a coherent sheaf on a noetherian scheme +
168	Snapper's polynomial-eventually-property are in scope. For the iter-176
169	file-skeleton the body is a typed `sorry`. -/
170	noncomputable def hilbertPolynomial {S X : Scheme.{u}} [IsLocallyNoetherian S]
171	    (_π : X ⟶ S) [LocallyOfFiniteType _π] (_L _F : X.Modules) (_s : S) :
172	    Polynomial ℚ :=
173	  sorry
174	
175	/-! ## §2. The Quot functor
176	
177	The Quot functor `Quot^{Φ,L}_{E/X/S}` sends an `S`-scheme `T ⟶ S` to the
178	set of equivalence classes `⟨F, q⟩` of pairs `(F, q)` where
179	- `F` is a coherent sheaf on `X_T = X ×_S T` whose schematic support is
180	  proper over `T` and which is `T`-flat,
181	- `q : E_T ↠ F` is a surjective `O_{X_T}`-linear homomorphism,
182	- the fiberwise Hilbert polynomial of `F|_{X_t}` with respect to `L|_{X_t}`
183	  equals `Φ` at every `t ∈ T`.
184	
185	Two pairs `(F, q)` and `(F', q')` are equivalent iff `ker(q) = ker(q')`.
186	
187	The Hilbert scheme is the special case `E = O_X`:
188	`Hilb^{Φ,L}_{X/S} = Quot^{Φ,L}_{O_X/X/S}`.
189	
190	Blueprint reference: `def:quot_functor` (Nitsure §1; FGA Explained Ch. 5). -/
191	
192	/-- The **Quot functor** `Quot^{Φ,L}_{E/X/S}` of coherent quotients of `E`
193	on `X ×_S -` with Hilbert polynomial `Φ`.
194	
195	Encoded as a contravariant functor `(Over S)ᵒᵖ ⥤ Type u`, sending an
196	`S`-scheme `T → S` (i.e. an object of `Over S`) to the set of
197	equivalence classes `⟨F, q⟩` of pairs `(F, q)` of a `T`-flat coherent
198	sheaf `F` on `X ×_S T` with proper support and a surjection
199	`q : E_T ↠ F` whose fiberwise Hilbert polynomial is `Φ`, modulo
200	`ker(q) = ker(q')`. Functoriality is pullback of the quotient along
201	`X ×_S T' ⟶ X ×_S T`.
202	
203	iter-177+: the body packages the on-objects / on-morphisms data using the
204	`Scheme.Modules.pullback` bifunctor on the relative product
205	`X ×_S T`, with the equivalence relation `ker(q) = ker(q')` quotiented
206	out via `Setoid` / `Quotient`. For the iter-176 file-skeleton the body
207	is a typed `sorry`. -/
208	noncomputable def QuotFunctor {S X : Scheme.{u}} [IsLocallyNoetherian S]
209	    (_π : X ⟶ S) [LocallyOfFiniteType _π] (_L _E : X.Modules)
210	    (_Φ : Polynomial ℚ) :
211	    (Over S)ᵒᵖ ⥤ Type u :=
212	  sorry
213	
214	end Scheme
215	
216	/-! ## §3. The Grassmannian scheme
217	
218	Since Mathlib carries no Grassmannian *scheme*, we encode it here as a
219	contravariant functor on `Over S` together with a representability
220	statement. The construction proceeds by gluing `binom(r, d)` affine
221	charts `U^I ≅ A^{d(r-d)}_S` along the Plücker cocycle, yielding a smooth
222	projective `S`-scheme `Gr_S(V, d)` of relative dimension `d(r-d)`,
223	equipped with a tautological rank-`d` quotient
224	`π* V ↠ U` and the Plücker closed embedding into `ℙ_S(⋀^d V)`.
225	
226	Blueprint references: `def:grassmannian_scheme`,
227	`thm:grassmannian_representable` (Nitsure §1 Exercise (2),

250	/-- **Representability of the Grassmannian.**
251	
252	For a noetherian scheme `S`, a locally free `O_S`-module `V` of rank `r`,

255	`Gr_S(V, d) ⟶ S` of relative dimension `d(r-d)`, equipped with a

257	`det(U)` is relatively very ample, giving a Plücker closed embedding
258	`Gr_S(V, d) ↪ ℙ_S(⋀^d V)`.

277	/-! ## §4. Representability of the Quot scheme
278	
279	Grothendieck–Altman–Kleiman: for a noetherian `S`, a projective
280	`π : X ⟶ S`, a relatively very ample `L` on `X`, a coherent
281	`E` on `X`, and `Φ ∈ ℚ[λ]`, the Quot functor `Quot^{Φ,L}_{E/X/S}` is
282	representable by a *projective* `S`-scheme.
283	
284	The proof has four steps (Nitsure §5):
285	1. **Boundedness** via Castelnuovo–Mumford `m`-regularity (uniform across
286	   fibers of `π` and across coherent quotients of `E_s` with Hilbert
287	   polynomial `Φ`).
288	2. **Grassmannian embedding**
289	   `α : Quot^{Φ,L}_{E/X/S} ↪ Grass(W ⊗_{O_S} Sym^r V, Φ(r))`
290	   for `r ≥ m`, sending `⟨F, q⟩ ↦ ⟨(π_T)_* F(r), (π_T)_*(q(r))⟩`.
291	3. **Locally closed in Grassmannian** via the flattening stratification
292	   applied to the universal cokernel on the Grassmannian, producing the
293	   stratum `T_0^Φ`.
294	4. **Closed embedding** by the valuative criterion of properness for
295	   DVRs.
296	
297	The reduction to the universal case `X = ℙ(V)`, `E = π*W` is recorded as
298	`lem:quot_reduction_to_pi_star_W` in the blueprint chapter.
299	
300	Blueprint reference: `thm:quot_representable` (Nitsure §5; FGA Explained
301	Ch. 5; Grothendieck, FGA TDTE-IV). -/
302	
303	/-- **Representability of the Quot scheme** (Grothendieck, Altman–Kleiman).
304	
305	Let `S` be a noetherian scheme, `π : X ⟶ S` a projective morphism (here
306	encoded as a proper `LocallyOfFiniteType` morphism; the projectivity
307	upgrades once `IsProjective` lands in Mathlib), `L` a line bundle on `X`
308	(relatively very ample), `E` a coherent `O_X`-module, and
309	`Φ ∈ ℚ[λ]`. Then the Quot functor `Quot^{Φ,L}_{E/X/S}` of `QuotFunctor`
310	is representable by an `S`-scheme.
311	
312	We package the conclusion as the existence of `Q : Over S` together with
313	a `Functor.RepresentableBy Q` witness for `QuotFunctor π L E Φ`; the
314	*projectivity* of `Q ⟶ S` (and the universal quotient
315	`q^univ : π^*_Q E ↠ F^univ` on `X ×_S Q^{Φ,L}`) is implicit in the
316	construction (Plücker-embedded into a projective Grassmannian over `S`)
317	and is iter-177+ refinement work.
318	
319	iter-177+: the body follows the four-step Nitsure §5 proof
320	(boundedness ⟶ Grassmannian embedding ⟶ flattening stratification ⟶
321	valuative-criterion closed embedding); the sub-lemmas live in
322	`lem:quot_boundedness`, `lem:quot_alpha_injective`,
323	`lem:quot_valuative_criterion`, and the existential reduction in
324	`lem:quot_reduction_to_pi_star_W`. For the iter-176 file-skeleton the
325	body is a typed `sorry`. -/
326	theorem QuotScheme {S X : Scheme.{u}} [IsLocallyNoetherian S]
327	    (π : X ⟶ S) [LocallyOfFiniteType π] [IsProper π]
328	    (L E : X.Modules) (Φ : Polynomial ℚ) :
329	    ∃ (Q : Over S), Nonempty ((QuotFunctor π L E Φ).RepresentableBy Q) := by
330	  sorry

334	/-! ## §5. Cohomology and base change
335	
336	The Quot construction uses cohomology-and-base-change in two places: the
337	boundedness step (Nitsure §5 "Use of m-Regularity") and the Grassmannian
338	embedding (Nitsure §5 "Embedding Quot into Grassmannian"). We record the
339	relevant statement as a named theorem so the Lean encoding can cite it
340	directly.
341	
342	The Stacks 02KH form is the statement for higher direct images
343	`R^i f_*` on quasi-coherent sheaves; for the iter-176 file-skeleton we
344	state the `i = 0` form on `Scheme.Modules`, which is the substantive
345	content of `lemma-flat-base-change-cohomology(ii)` of Stacks 02KH. The
346	`R^i` form is iter-177+ work after higher-direct-image infrastructure
347	is in scope.
348	
349	Blueprint reference: `thm:flat_base_change_cohomology` (Stacks 02KH). -/
350	
351	/-! ### Flat base change of cohomology (Stacks tag 02KH, `i = 0` form)
352	
353	Let
354	```
355	   g'
356	X' ───→ X
357	│       │
358	f'      f
359	↓       ↓
360	S' ───→ S
361	   g
362	```
363	be a cartesian square of schemes with `g` flat and `f` quasi-compact
364	quasi-separated. Let `F` be a sheaf of `O_X`-modules. Then the canonical
365	base-change map `g* (f_* F) ⟶ f'_* ((g')* F)` is an isomorphism in
366	`S'.Modules`.
367	
368	(The full Stacks 02KH statement covers all higher direct images
369	`R^i f_* F` for `i ≥ 0`; the `i = 0` form encoded here is the
370	substantive content of `lemma-flat-base-change-cohomology(ii)` of
371	Stacks 02KH, with the `i ≥ 1` form post-iter-177 work after the
372	higher-direct-image bifunctor lands.)
373	
374	iter-177 (Lane QS-FLAT): the body constructs the canonical base-change
375	natural transformation via the mate equivalence of the
376	`pullback ⊣ pushforward` adjunction (Mathlib's `mateEquiv` of
377	`Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction`), then exhibits the iso
378	via the `canonicalBaseChangeMap_isIso` helper. The deep mathematical
379	content (Stacks tag 02KH / 02KE / 00H8) lives entirely in the helper;
380	it reduces affine-locally to: for a flat ring map `A → B` and an
381	`A`-algebra `R`, the canonical map `B ⊗_A H^i(X, F) → H^i(X_B, F_B)`
382	is an iso for any quasi-coherent `F` (the `i = 0` form is what we use).
383	The helper remains a typed `sorry` pending the affine-local reduction
384	+ algebraic flat base change; this is iter-178+ body work after
385	quasi-compact open-cover Mayer-Vietoris infrastructure is in scope. -/
386	
387	/-- The canonical base-change natural transformation `g* (f_* -) ⟶ f'_* ((g')* -)`
388	associated to a cartesian square
389	```
390	     g'
391	X' ─────→ X
392	│         │
393	f'        f
394	↓         ↓
395	S' ─────→ S
396	     g
397	```
398	in `Scheme`. Constructed as the *mate* (Beck–Chevalley transform)
399	under the `pullback ⊣ pushforward` adjunctions on sheaves of modules
400	of the canonical 2-isomorphism
401	`pullback g ⋙ pullback f' ≅ pullback f ⋙ pullback g'` coming from
402	`g' ≫ f = f' ≫ g`.
403	
404	This natural transformation always exists (no flatness needed). The
405	content of the flat base-change theorem (Stacks tag 02KH) is the
406	*isomorphism* claim under the hypotheses
407	`[QuasiCompact f] [QuasiSeparated f] [Flat g]`; that claim is the
408	helper `canonicalBaseChangeMap_isIso`. -/
409	noncomputable def canonicalBaseChangeMap
410	    {X X' S S' : Scheme.{u}}
411	    {f : X ⟶ S} {g : S' ⟶ S} {g' : X' ⟶ X} {f' : X' ⟶ S'}
412	    (sq : IsPullback g' f' f g) :
413	    Scheme.Modules.pushforward f ⋙ Scheme.Modules.pullback g ⟶
414	      Scheme.Modules.pullback g' ⋙ Scheme.Modules.pushforward f' :=
415	  CategoryTheory.mateEquiv
416	      (Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction f)
417	      (Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction f')
418	      (((Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp f' g) ≪≫
419	        Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr sq.w.symm ≪≫
420	        (Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp g' f).symm).hom)
421	
422	/-- **Section-wise form of flat base change** (Stacks tag 02KH(ii)).
423	
424	For every open `U` of `S'`, the section over `U` of the canonical base-change
425	map `(pullback g).obj ((pushforward f).obj F) ⟶ (pushforward f').obj ((pullback g').obj F)`
426	is an isomorphism.
427	
428	This is the substantive content of Stacks 02KH(ii) (the `i = 0` form):
429	* on quasi-compact opens `U ⊆ S'` it reduces to the algebraic flat base
430	  change `B ⊗_A H⁰(X, F) ≅ H⁰(X_B, F_B)` (Stacks 00H8 / 02KE), via
431	  `Module.Flat.isBaseChange` on the ring map `A = Γ(S, V) → B = Γ(S', V')`
432	  for an affine open `V ⊆ S` with `V' = g⁻¹V`;
433	* the general case follows by Mayer-Vietoris from the quasi-separation of
434	  `f` (so intersections of preimages are quasi-compact, hence the algebraic
435	  step applies to each pair of affine charts).
436	
437	iter-178+: the body of this helper is the deep mathematical content of
438	flat base change of cohomology. It requires both
439	(i) the affine-local identification of `Scheme.Modules` sections of
440	    `(pullback g).obj ((pushforward f).obj F)` over an affine open `U`
441	    with the algebraic tensor product `Γ(S', U) ⊗_{Γ(S, V)} Γ(X_V, F|_{X_V})`,
442	    and
443	(ii) an open-cover Mayer-Vietoris on `S'.Modules` sections.
444	Neither piece is in scope at the pinned Mathlib commit. The body is a
445	typed `sorry` carrying the *intended* mathematical content
446	(Stacks 02KH(ii)). -/
447	theorem canonicalBaseChangeMap_app_app_isIso {X X' S S' : Scheme.{u}}
448	    {f : X ⟶ S} {g : S' ⟶ S} {g' : X' ⟶ X} {f' : X' ⟶ S'}
449	    (sq : IsPullback g' f' f g)
450	    [QuasiCompact f] [QuasiSeparated f] [Flat g]
451	    (F : X.Modules) (U : S'.Opens) :
452	    IsIso (((canonicalBaseChangeMap sq).app F).app U) := by
453	  -- Stacks 02KH(ii) (i = 0, section form). Proof strategy (iter-178+):
454	  --   1. Pick an affine open `V ⊆ S` together with a quasi-compact open
455	  --      `V' = g⁻¹V ⊆ S'`. On `V'` the section of
456	  --      `(pullback g).obj ((pushforward f).obj F)` identifies with
457	  --      `Γ(S', V') ⊗_{Γ(S, V)} Γ(X, f⁻¹V, F)`.
458	  --   2. The section of `(pushforward f').obj ((pullback g').obj F)` on `V'`
459	  --      identifies with `Γ(X', (f')⁻¹V', F')` where `F' = (g')* F`.
460	  --   3. The algebraic flat base change `Module.Flat.isBaseChange` on the
461	  --      ring map `Γ(S, V) → Γ(S', V')` (flat by `Flat g`) gives the iso
462	  --      on the affine open `V'`.
463	  --   4. Quasi-separated `f` ⟹ `f⁻¹V ∩ f⁻¹V₀` is quasi-compact for any
464	  --      pair of affine opens, so Mayer-Vietoris glues the affine-local
465	  --      iso to an iso on the whole `U`.
466	  -- Pending: project-side sub-build identifying `Scheme.Modules` sections
467	  -- with explicit tensor products of `Γ(X, F)` modules, plus an
468	  -- open-cover Mayer-Vietoris on quasi-compact quasi-separated targets.
469	  sorry
470	
471	/-- **Flat base-change is an isomorphism** (Stacks tag 02KH, `i = 0`).
472	
473	The canonical base-change natural transformation `canonicalBaseChangeMap`
474	is an isomorphism at every coherent sheaf `F` under the hypotheses
475	`[QuasiCompact f]`, `[QuasiSeparated f]`, `[Flat g]`.
476	
477	The proof reduces section-wise via `Scheme.Modules.Hom.isIso_iff_isIso_app`
478	to the section-form helper `canonicalBaseChangeMap_app_app_isIso`,
479	which captures Stacks 02KH(ii) — the substantive algebraic content
480	(`Module.Flat.isBaseChange` on each affine open + Mayer-Vietoris for
481	quasi-separated `f`). -/
482	theorem canonicalBaseChangeMap_isIso {X X' S S' : Scheme.{u}}
483	    {f : X ⟶ S} {g : S' ⟶ S} {g' : X' ⟶ X} {f' : X' ⟶ S'}
484	    (sq : IsPullback g' f' f g)
485	    [QuasiCompact f] [QuasiSeparated f] [Flat g]
486	    (F : X.Modules) :
487	    IsIso ((canonicalBaseChangeMap sq).app F) :=
488	  Scheme.Modules.Hom.isIso_iff_isIso_app.mpr
489	    (fun U => canonicalBaseChangeMap_app_app_isIso sq F U)
490	
491	theorem flatBaseChangeCohomology {X X' S S' : Scheme.{u}}
492	    {f : X ⟶ S} {g : S' ⟶ S} {g' : X' ⟶ X} {f' : X' ⟶ S'}
493	    (sq : IsPullback g' f' f g)
494	    [QuasiCompact f] [QuasiSeparated f] [Flat g]
495	    (F : X.Modules) :
496	    Nonempty ((Scheme.Modules.pullback g).obj
497	                ((Scheme.Modules.pushforward f).obj F) ≅
498	              (Scheme.Modules.pushforward f').obj
499	                ((Scheme.Modules.pullback g').obj F)) :=
500	  -- Build the canonical Beck-Chevalley base-change map and wrap it in `asIso`
501	  -- using the iso-claim from `canonicalBaseChangeMap_isIso`.
502	  ⟨@asIso _ _ _ _ _ (canonicalBaseChangeMap_isIso sq F)⟩
503	
504	end AlgebraicGeometry

740	\section{Cohomology and base change}
741	\label{sec:cohomology_base_change}
742	
743	The Quot construction uses cohomology-and-base-change in two places: the
744	boundedness step (\cref{lem:quot_boundedness}) and the Grassmannian
745	embedding (\cref{lem:quot_alpha_injective}). We record the relevant
746	statement as a named theorem so the Lean encoding can cite it directly.
747	
748	\begin{theorem}\leanok
749	[Flat base change of cohomology]
750	  \label{thm:flat_base_change_cohomology}
751	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.flatBaseChangeCohomology}
752	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], tag 02KH (lemma-flat-base-change-cohomology)
753	  % (read from references/stacks-coherent.tex, L947-L970).
754	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
755	  % "Consider a cartesian diagram of schemes
756	  %  $$
757	  %  \xymatrix{
758	  %  X' \ar[d]_{f'} \ar[r]_{g'} & X \ar[d]^f \\
759	  %  S' \ar[r]^g & S
760	  %  }
761	  %  $$
762	  %  Let $\mathcal{F}$ be a quasi-coherent $\mathcal{O}_X$-module
763	  %  with pullback $\mathcal{F}' = (g')^*\mathcal{F}$.
764	  %  Assume that $g$ is flat and that $f$ is quasi-compact and quasi-separated.
765	  %  For any $i \geq 0$
766	  %  \begin{enumerate}
767	  %  \item the base change map of
768	  %  Cohomology, Lemma \ref{cohomology-lemma-base-change-map-flat-case}
769	  %  is an isomorphism
770	  %  $$
771	  %  g^*R^if_*\mathcal{F} \longrightarrow R^if'_*\mathcal{F}',
772	  %  $$
773	  %  \item if $S = \Spec(A)$ and $S' = \Spec(B)$, then
774	  %  $H^i(X, \mathcal{F}) \otimes_A B = H^i(X', \mathcal{F}')$.
775	  %  \end{enumerate}"
776	  \textit{Source: [Stacks Project], tag 02KH (lemma-flat-base-change-cohomology).}
777	  Let
778	  \[
779	    \begin{array}{ccc}
780	      X' & \xrightarrow{g'} & X \\
781	      \downarrow f' &  & \downarrow f \\
782	      S' & \xrightarrow{g} & S
783	    \end{array}
784	  \]
785	  be a cartesian diagram of schemes with $g$ flat and $f$ quasi-compact and
786	  quasi-separated. Let $\mathcal{F}$ be a quasi-coherent
787	  $\mathcal{O}_X$-module with pull-back
788	  $\mathcal{F}' = (g')^* \mathcal{F}$. For every $i \ge 0$:
789	  \begin{enumerate}
790	    \item the base-change map
791	      $g^* R^i f_* \mathcal{F} \to R^i f'_* \mathcal{F}'$ is an isomorphism;
792	    \item if $S = \Spec A$ and $S' = \Spec B$, then $H^i(X, \mathcal{F})
793	      \otimes_A B = H^i(X', \mathcal{F}')$.
794	  \end{enumerate}
795	\end{theorem}
796	
797	\section{Lean encoding}
798	\label{sec:quot_lean_encoding}
799	
800	The Lean target file is the new
801	\texttt{AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/QuotScheme.lean}. The construction
802	proceeds in three phases.
803	
804	\paragraph{Phase 1: Hilbert polynomial (\cref{def:hilbert_polynomial}).}
805	On a finite-type morphism $\pi : X \to S$ with $S$ noetherian and a
806	coherent $\mathcal{F}$ on $X$ with proper support over $S$, the per-fiber
807	Hilbert polynomial is encoded as a function $s \mapsto
808	\Phi_{\mathcal{F},s} \in \mathbb{Q}[\lambda]$. Mathlib provides
809	\texttt{CategoryTheory.PolynomialModule} and Euler-characteristic
810	infrastructure on coherent sheaves on noetherian schemes; the polynomial
811	property of $m \mapsto \chi(\mathcal{F} \otimes L^{\otimes m})$ requires
812	Snapper's Lemma, which is a project-side sub-build on top of the
813	graded-Euler-characteristic infrastructure.
814	
815	\paragraph{Phase 2: Grassmannian scheme (\cref{def:grassmannian_scheme},
816	\cref{thm:grassmannian_representable}).} The Grassmannian
817	$\mathrm{Gr}_S(V, d)$ is encoded as the relative gluing of $\binom{r}{d}$
818	affine charts $U^I$ (each isomorphic to a relative affine space
819	$\mathbb{A}^{d(r-d)}_S$) via the Pl\"ucker cocycle $\theta_{I,J}$. The
820	gluing backbone is \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.GlueData}. The
821	universal quotient sheaf $\mathcal{U}$ is glued by the transition cocycle
822	$g_{I,J} = (X^I_J)^{-1}$, and the determinant line bundle
823	$\det \mathcal{U}$ gives the Pl\"ucker closed embedding into
824	$\mathbb{P}_S(\bigwedge^d V)$. The representability statement uses
825	\texttt{CategoryTheory.Functor.RepresentableBy} (as in
826	\cref{chap:Picard_RelativeSpec}) on the universal pair
827	$(\mathrm{Gr}_S(V, d), \mathcal{U})$.
828	
829	\paragraph{Phase 3: Quot scheme (\cref{thm:quot_representable}).}
830	The Quot functor is constructed as a locally closed subfunctor of the
831	Grassmannian
832	$\mathrm{Grass}(W \otimes_{\mathcal{O}_S} \mathrm{Sym}^r V, \Phi(r))$, via:
833	\begin{enumerate}
834	  \item the uniform $m$-regularity bound from
835	    \cref{lem:quot_boundedness} (input: Castelnuovo--Mumford theorem,
836	    \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.CastelnuovoMumford} -- project-side sub-build);
837	  \item the Grassmannian embedding $\alpha$ from
838	    \cref{lem:quot_alpha_injective};
839	  \item the flattening stratification (\cref{thm:flattening_stratification_exists}
840	    of \cref{chap:Picard_FlatteningStratification}, A.2.a) applied to the
841	    universal quotient on the Grassmannian, producing the locally closed
842	    stratum $T_0^\Phi$;
843	  \item the valuative-criterion upgrade
844	    (\cref{lem:quot_valuative_criterion}) to closed embedding, hence
845	    projective $S$-scheme.
846	\end{enumerate}
847	The reduction \cref{lem:quot_reduction_to_pi_star_W} from the universal
848	case to the general case is encoded as a closed embedding of representable
849	subfunctors via the kernel-vanishing scheme.
850	
851	\paragraph{Cohomology and base change.}
852	\cref{thm:flat_base_change_cohomology} is the Mathlib-equivalent of
853	Stacks tag 02KH. Mathlib carries a partial form
854	(\texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.flat\_base\_change}) for affine bases; the
855	full $R^i f_*$ statement is a project-side bridge if not present at the
856	pinned commit.
857	
858	\paragraph{Dependencies summary.}
859	This file directly consumes
860	\cref{chap:Picard_RelativeSpec} (A.1.a, on disk) for relative-spectrum
861	gluing infrastructure and
862	\cref{thm:flattening_stratification_exists} from
863	\cref{chap:Picard_FlatteningStratification} (A.2.a, written this iter)
864	for the flattening stratum. Downstream, \cref{chap:Picard_FGAPicRepresentability}
865	(A.2.c) consumes \cref{thm:quot_representable} via the special case
866	$E = \mathcal{O}_C$ on a smooth proper curve $C/k$, giving the Hilbert
867	scheme $\Hilb_{C/k} = \Quot^{\Phi,\mathcal{O}_C(1)}_{\mathcal{O}_C/C/k}$
868	and its open subscheme $\Div_{C/k}$ of relative effective divisors.
869	
870	\section{Out of scope}
871	\label{sec:quot_out_of_scope}
872	
873	This chapter packages \emph{only} the Quot-scheme existence statement and
874	its proof skeleton via the Grassmannian + flattening-stratification
875	route. The following are explicitly out of scope here:
876	\begin{itemize}
877	  \item \textbf{Flattening stratification} (A.2.a) -- consumed-by, not
878	    proved-here. Its blueprint home is the new chapter
879	    \texttt{Picard\_FlatteningStratification.tex}.
880	  \item \textbf{Hilbert scheme as a separate chapter}. The Hilbert scheme
881	    $\Hilb_{X/S}$ is the special case
882	    $\Hilb_{X/S} = \Quot_{\mathcal{O}_X/X/S}$ of the Quot scheme and is
883	    introduced inline (\cref{def:quot_functor}); no separate chapter.
884	  \item \textbf{Castelnuovo--Mumford $m$-regularity theorem} -- input to
885	    \cref{lem:quot_boundedness}, not proved here. The proof lives in
886	    Nitsure \S 2; for the Lean encoding it is a project-side sub-build.
887	  \item \textbf{Semi-continuity and base change} (Nitsure \S 3) -- the
888	    flat-base-change theorem of cohomology (\cref{thm:flat_base_change_cohomology})
889	    is stated here as a lemma, but the upstream Mathlib bridge / project-side
890	    sub-build is not constructed in this chapter.
891	  \item \textbf{Snapper's Lemma} -- the polynomial-eventually property of
892	    Euler characteristics, used in \cref{def:hilbert_polynomial}. Cited
893	    via Nitsure \S 1 and (textbook reference) Hartshorne III.5.2;

420	        (Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp g' f).symm).hom)
421	
422	/-- **Section-wise form of flat base change** (Stacks tag 02KH(ii)).
423	
424	For every open `U` of `S'`, the section over `U` of the canonical base-change
425	map `(pullback g).obj ((pushforward f).obj F) ⟶ (pushforward f').obj ((pullback g').obj F)`
426	is an isomorphism.
427	
428	This is the substantive content of Stacks 02KH(ii) (the `i = 0` form):
429	* on quasi-compact opens `U ⊆ S'` it reduces to the algebraic flat base
430	  change `B ⊗_A H⁰(X, F) ≅ H⁰(X_B, F_B)` (Stacks 00H8 / 02KE), via
431	  `Module.Flat.isBaseChange` on the ring map `A = Γ(S, V) → B = Γ(S', V')`
432	  for an affine open `V ⊆ S` with `V' = g⁻¹V`;
433	* the general case follows by Mayer-Vietoris from the quasi-separation of
434	  `f` (so intersections of preimages are quasi-compact, hence the algebraic
435	  step applies to each pair of affine charts).
436	
437	iter-178+: the body of this helper is the deep mathematical content of
438	flat base change of cohomology. It requires both
439	(i) the affine-local identification of `Scheme.Modules` sections of
440	    `(pullback g).obj ((pushforward f).obj F)` over an affine open `U`
441	    with the algebraic tensor product `Γ(S', U) ⊗_{Γ(S, V)} Γ(X_V, F|_{X_V})`,
442	    and
443	(ii) an open-cover Mayer-Vietoris on `S'.Modules` sections.
444	Neither piece is in scope at the pinned Mathlib commit. The body is a
445	typed `sorry` carrying the *intended* mathematical content
446	(Stacks 02KH(ii)). -/
447	theorem canonicalBaseChangeMap_app_app_isIso {X X' S S' : Scheme.{u}}
448	    {f : X ⟶ S} {g : S' ⟶ S} {g' : X' ⟶ X} {f' : X' ⟶ S'}
449	    (sq : IsPullback g' f' f g)
450	    [QuasiCompact f] [QuasiSeparated f] [Flat g]
451	    (F : X.Modules) (U : S'.Opens) :
452	    IsIso (((canonicalBaseChangeMap sq).app F).app U) := by
453	  -- Stacks 02KH(ii) (i = 0, section form). Proof strategy (iter-178+):
454	  --   1. Pick an affine open `V ⊆ S` together with a quasi-compact open
455	  --      `V' = g⁻¹V ⊆ S'`. On `V'` the section of
456	  --      `(pullback g).obj ((pushforward f).obj F)` identifies with
457	  --      `Γ(S', V') ⊗_{Γ(S, V)} Γ(X, f⁻¹V, F)`.

Restriction of `canonicalBaseChangeMap_app_app_isIso` to the case where the

flat base-change map of `Module.Flat.isBaseChange` (Mathlib's

`Flat g`.

  reports `[propext, sorryAx, Classical.choice, Quot.sound]` — only

  introduced.

   (line 446 in the post-edit file): affine-open case. Intended body uses
   `Module.Flat.isBaseChange` on the ring map `Γ(S, V) → Γ(S', U)`

   `f`. Substantive Mathlib gap; the abstract sheaf-theoretic principle

   piece owed alongside the affine identification.

### What's substantive in each helper

- **Helper 1**: deep mathematical content of `H⁰`-form flat base change.
  Requires three sub-bridges all currently absent at the pinned Mathlib

  Requires the project-side `Sheaf.Hom.isIso_iff_isIso_on_basis` for

- `Scheme.Modules.Hom.isIso_iff_isIso_app` — used by the existing

1	# Archon Project
2	
3	You are one of: the plan agent, a prover agent, a subagent (per descriptor in `.archon/subagents/`), or the review agent. Read `PROGRESS.md` to determine your role and current objectives. Keep workspace tidy. Prefer existing MCP tools.
4	
5	## Priority Rule
6	
7	When instructions conflict between global and local sources, **local takes precedence**:
8	
9	- Prompts in `.archon/prompts/` override Archon's global prompts.
10	- Skills in `.claude/skills/` override globally installed plugins.
11	- Rules in `.claude/rules/` apply only to this project.
12	
13	## Skills
14	
15	- **archon-lean4** — installed as `lean4@archon-local` plugin (live-linked to Archon source). Provides `/archon-lean4:prove`, `/archon-lean4:golf`, `/archon-lean4:doctor`, etc.
16	
17	## Tools
18	
19	Project tools live in `.claude/tools/` as directly-executable scripts. List with `ls .claude/tools/`; run `<path> --help`. MCP servers (Lean LSP, etc.) are registered in `.claude/settings.json` / `.mcp.json` and surface as `mcp__*` tools.
20	
21	Two always-present scripts:
22	
23	- **`archon-informal-agent.py`** — call external LLMs (OpenAI/Gemini/OpenRouter) for an informal proof-style sketch when you're stuck on an approach and want a second opinion. **Requires `OPENAI_API_KEY` / `GEMINI_API_KEY` / `OPENROUTER_API_KEY` in env** — check via `env | grep -E "OPENAI|GEMINI|OPENROUTER"` BEFORE planning around its use; if no key is set, fall back to alternatives below. The output is LLM-generated from training data, NOT source-derived: this tool is **not** a literature retriever. For actual literature lookup (cross-check a claim against Hartshorne, fetch an arXiv paper, summarize a Stacks tag) consult the auto-injected subagent catalog — when a literature/reference-fetching subagent is enabled it will appear there — or use the `WebSearch` / `WebFetch` tools directly.
24	- **`archon-subagent.py`** — the generic subagent dispatcher (one wrapper handles every subagent — see "Subagents" below).
25	
26	## Subagents
27	
28	Subagents are descriptor files at `.archon/subagents/<name>.md` (YAML frontmatter — `name`, `description`, `write_domain`, `read_only`, `can_spawn`, `default_enabled`, optional `mandatory: [<phase>...]`, optional `dispatcher_notes` — followed by the prompt body the spawned Claude reads).
29	
30	**You do NOT need to discover subagents.** The plan and review prompts auto-inject an **Available subagents** section at the top of each invocation listing every enabled descriptor with description, write-domain, MANDATORY flag, and `dispatcher_notes`. When you decide to invoke a subagent, read its full prompt at `.archon/subagents/<name>.md`.
31	
32	**Invoke** via the generic wrapper (Bash tool, foreground):
33	
34	```
35	python3 .claude/tools/archon-subagent.py \
36	  --name <subagent-name> \
37	  --slug <kebab-slug> \
38	  --directive-file <path-to-directive.md> \
39	  --write-domain '<glob>'        # repeat for multiple
40	```
41	
42	The wrapper exits 0 on success, prints a one-line status, writes the report to `.archon/task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`, and the Archon CLI auto-archives a copy to `logs/iter-NNN/<name>-<slug>-report.md` for the dashboard. The dispatch semaphore caps concurrent subagent processes at `loop.max_parallel`; the wrapper resolves `--parent-slug` from `ARCHON_SUBAGENT_SLUG` so nested children inherit the hierarchy.
43	
44	**Treat each dispatch as blocking — wait for its report before acting on it.** Don't *deliberately* set `run_in_background: true`. But note the wrapper is genuinely synchronous (it `subprocess.run`s the `archon subagent` CLI and only returns once the child finishes and its report is on disk), so the harness may *auto-background* a long-running dispatch and hand you a task ID immediately — that's expected and fine. When it does, await that task / poll for the report file at `.archon/task_results/<name>-<slug>.md` (the wrapper's one-line status and exit 0 confirm completion) before you read or act on the result. The thing to avoid is firing a dispatch and then continuing to plan as if it had already returned; whether the call blocked inline or was auto-backgrounded, the rule is the same: do not consume a subagent's output until its report exists.
45	
46	**Highly-recommended subagents.** A descriptor whose frontmatter sets `mandatory: [plan]` is treated as **highly recommended** for the plan phase (similarly `[review]` for the review phase). The catalog tags them `[HIGHLY RECOMMENDED]`. Dispatch each one unless you have a concrete reason to skip — when skipping, record a one-line rationale under a `## Subagent skips` section in `iter/iter-NNN/<phase>.md` (e.g. `- strategy-critic: STRATEGY.md SHA unchanged from prior iter and last verdict was SOUND`). A post-phase audit checks both signals: it silences when the subagent dispatched OR a skip rationale was recorded, and warns only when neither happened. Each subagent's `dispatcher_notes` enumerates its specific skip conditions — read them before deciding. **Subagents ship disabled by default** (`default_enabled: false`), so the rule only fires for subagents the user has explicitly listed under `subagents.enabled` in `.archon/config.json`; on a fresh project the catalog is empty and no recommended dispatch is required. (The frontmatter field is still named `mandatory: [...]` for backward compat with existing configs and descriptors — its semantic meaning is "highly recommended", not "must dispatch".)
47	
48	## Key Files & Permissions
49	
50	All Archon state files are in `.archon/`. The table below covers **phase agents** (plan, prover, review) and the **user**. Subagent permissions are NOT a fixed row — they are descriptor-driven (`write_domain` glob in the subagent's frontmatter, enforced at dispatch time). See "Subagent permissions" after the table.
51	
52	| File | Plan | Prover | Review | User |
53	|------|------|--------|--------|------|
54	| `PROGRESS.md` | read + write | read only | read only | read |
55	| `STRATEGY.md` | read + write | — | — | read |
56	| `USER_HINTS.md` | **loop-managed** (captured + cleared by the CLI; injected into the plan prompt) | — | — | write |
57	| `task_pending.md` | read + write | read only | read only | read |
58	| `task_done.md` | read + write | read only | read only | read |
59	| `task_results/<file>.md` | read (collect) | write (own file) | read only | read |
60	| `proof-journal/` | read | — | **write** | read |
61	| `PROJECT_STATUS.md` | read | — | **write** (Knowledge Base only — session log lives in `iter/iter-NNN/review.md`) | read |
62	| `iter/iter-NNN/plan.md` | **write** (this iter only) | — | read (recent window injected) | read |
63	| `iter/iter-NNN/review.md` | read (recent window injected) | — | **write** (this iter only) | read |
64	| `iter/iter-NNN/objectives.md` | optional write | — | read | read |
65	| `archon-protected.yaml` | read | read | read | **write** |
66	| `.lean` files | — | write (own file; frozen protected signatures) | — | write (via comments) |
67	| `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex` | **write** (informal prose, `\lean{...}` hints) | — | **write** (semantic markers: `\mathlibok`, `% NOTE:`, `\lean{...}` corrections — NOT `\leanok`) | read |
68	| `analogies/<slug>.md` | read (when relevant) | — | — | read |
69	| `TO_USER.md` | — (review owns) | — | **write** (reset at review start) | read |
70	
71	### Subagent permissions
72	
73	Each subagent ships YAML frontmatter:
74	
75	- `write_domain: "<glob>"` — file pattern the subagent may write. Empty for purely read-only subagents (the dispatching call typically passes `--write-domain 'task_results/**'` so the subagent can still write its own report).
76	- `read_only: true | false` — when `true`, the subagent may only write its `task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`.
77	- `can_spawn: true | false` — when `true`, the subagent may dispatch children whose `write_domain` is a subset of its own.
78	
79	The dispatcher enforces `write_domain` at the filesystem level: any child whose declared domain is not a subset of the parent's is rejected before the child agent starts. Reads are restricted only by the descriptor's prompt body (e.g. fresh-context critics that are told not to read PROGRESS.md / STRATEGY.md).
80	
81	**`.archon/REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md`** is reserved for the interactive `archon refactor draft` / `archon refactor run` flow run by hand by the mathematician. The autonomous loop never reads or writes that file. To invoke a refactor-style subagent inside the loop, write the directive to a fresh tempfile under `.archon/logs/iter-NNN/<name>-<slug>-directive.md` and dispatch via the generic wrapper. Older state files (`STRATEGY.md`, `task_pending.md`, `PROGRESS.md`) may contain leftover references to the old REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md flow — treat those as historical noise.
82	
83	## Protected Declarations
84	
85	`archon-protected.yaml` at the project root lists declarations whose **signatures are frozen** by the mathematician. Rules:
86	
87	- **Plan / prover / review** agents: read-only on protected signatures. May fill proof bodies; may not rename, re-type, or reorder arguments.
88	- **Write-capable structural subagents** (per their `write_domain`): may *move* a protected declaration to a different file (name + signature preserved verbatim) and must then update the path key in `archon-protected.yaml`. They may NEVER rename / re-type / delete / re-sign a protected declaration.
89	- The YAML file itself is user-only. No agent adds or removes a declaration. The only allowed agent edit is updating an existing declaration's file path.
90	
91	## Blueprint Marker Vocabulary
92	
93	Two semantic markers:
94	
95	- `\leanok` — statement block: declaration is formalized (at least a `sorry` present). Proof block: proof closed, no `sorry`.
96	- `\mathlibok` — statement block: declaration is backed by Mathlib (re-export / alias); no Archon proof obligation remains.
97	
98	No marker means the block is unformalized. If a block fails to translate, leave it unmarked and annotate with a `% NOTE:` comment.
99	
100	**`\leanok` is managed deterministically by the `sync_leanok` phase** that runs between prover and review each iteration. It walks every chapter, looks up each `\lean{...}` declaration, runs `sorry_analyzer` + `lake env lean`, and adds/removes `\leanok` accordingly. Agents must NOT add or remove `\leanok` themselves.
101	
102	`\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, and `% NOTE:` annotations are the review agent's domain (they require semantic judgement). The plan agent writes informal prose and `\lean{...}` hints; provers never touch the blueprint.
103	
104	## User Interaction
105	
106	The user provides hints in two places:
107	
108	- **Strategic hints** → `.archon/USER_HINTS.md`. The Archon loop captures this file's content before each plan phase, injects it into the plan agent's prompt, and resets the file to its bundled template (an HTML-comment format guide + zero bullets) after the plan phase succeeds. The HTML-comment preamble is stripped before injection so a template-only file renders as "no hints" to the planner. The plan agent does NOT read or clear that file itself. Provers never see these hints.
109	- **File-specific hints** → `/- USER: ... -/` comments directly in `.lean` files. The prover that owns that file sees them naturally.
110	
111	## Agent Roles
112	
113	### Plan Agent
114	
115	- Read `.archon/prompts/plan.md` for full instructions.
116	- User hints, blueprint-doctor findings, recent iter sidecars, the subagent catalog, and the references summary are all pre-injected into your invocation prompt. You do not "go read" those files.
117	- Read `task_results/` to collect prover and subagent results, then update `task_pending.md` / `task_done.md`.
118	- Optionally invoke subagents (via the auto-injected catalog) before writing prover objectives. Subagent reports are auto-archived to `logs/iter-NNN/` by the Archon CLI.
119	- Write `PROGRESS.md` with objectives for the next prover round.
120	- Write informal prose in `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex`. Do NOT touch markers — `\leanok` is owned by the deterministic sync; `\mathlibok` / `% NOTE:` by the review agent.
121	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or fill sorries yourself.
122	
123	### Prover Agent
124	
125	- Read `PROGRESS.md` for your objectives (read only).
126	- Read the stage-specific prompt from `.archon/prompts/` (`prover-autoformalize.md` / `prover-prover.md` / `prover-polish.md`).
127	- Write results to `task_results/<your_file>.md`.
128	- Write only to the `.lean` file(s) you are assigned — never edit another agent's file.
129	- Check for `/- USER: ... -/` comments in your file for file-specific hints.
130	- Do NOT edit blueprint chapters. Flag in your task result which declarations are ready for which marker; the review agent applies them.
131	
132	### Subagents
133	
134	Subagents are descriptor-driven and discovered at runtime. The catalog is auto-injected into the plan and review prompts each phase (see "Subagents" above for the wrapper invocation). Each subagent reads only what its directive points at — never `PROGRESS.md` / `STRATEGY.md` / phase-agent state — and writes a self-contained report at `task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`. Descriptors with `can_spawn: true` may dispatch children (subject to the global `loop.max_parallel` cap).
135	
136	### Review Agent
137	
138	- Read `.archon/prompts/review.md` for full instructions.
139	- Read `proof-journal/current_session/attempts_raw.jsonl` for structured prover attempt data.
140	- Write the session journal to `proof-journal/sessions/session_N/` (`summary.md`, `milestones.jsonl`, `recommendations.md`).
141	- Update `PROJECT_STATUS.md` (Knowledge Base only — session narrative goes to `iter/iter-NNN/review.md`).
142	- Maintain the semantic blueprint markers — `\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, `% NOTE:` annotations, stale `\notready` removal. Do NOT touch `\leanok`.
143	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or modify `PROGRESS.md`.
144	
145	### Loop infrastructure (no agent role)
146	
147	These run automatically between phases:
148	
149	- **Iter sidecar init** — creates `.archon/iter/iter-NNN/` at iter start so plan + review have a stable destination for per-iter narrative. Top-level files (STRATEGY.md, PROJECT_STATUS.md, task_*.md) stay bounded.
150	- **`sync_leanok`** — runs between prover and review, deterministically updating `\leanok` markers based on actual sorry counts and compilation status.
151	- **`blueprint-doctor`** — runs between prover and review, deterministically detecting orphan chapters, broken `\ref` / `\uses` / `\proves`, and new `axiom` declarations. Findings are written to `logs/iter-NNN/blueprint-doctor.{md,json}` and inlined into the next plan agent's prompt.

1	# Prover — Prover Stage
2	
3	You fill `sorry` placeholders with complete proofs in your assigned `.lean` file.
4	
5	## Workflow
6	
7	1. Read `PROGRESS.md` for your objectives (read only).
8	2. Read `task_pending.md` for prior attempts, dead ends, relevant lemmas on your file.
9	3. Check the `.lean` file for `/- USER: ... -/` comments (file-specific user hints).
10	4. **Read the relevant blueprint chapter before writing Lean code.** The chapter holds the mathematical proof sketch you must align with. When stuck, re-reading the blueprint is often the fastest path forward.
11	5. Replace `sorry` with Lean proofs. Push as far as possible.
12	6. **Always save partial progress in the code.** If you can't fully close a sorry, leave your best attempt — commented-out steps, helper lemmas, partial `by` blocks with `sorry` at the stuck point. The file must still compile (use scoped `sorry` if needed), but your work must be visible for the next agent to continue from. NEVER revert to a bare `sorry` — that wastes the work.
13	7. Write your results to `task_results/<your_file>.md`.
14	
15	**Write permissions**: only your assigned `.lean` file(s) and `task_results/<your_file>.md`. Do NOT edit `PROGRESS.md`, `task_pending.md`, `task_done.md`, blueprint chapters, or other agents' files.
16	
17	## Protected declarations
18	
19	`archon-protected.yaml` lists declarations with **frozen signatures**. You may fill their proof bodies but must not rename, re-type, reorder arguments, or weaken hypotheses. Only the mathematician edits protected signatures.
20	
21	## Avoid early termination
22	
23	- Don't abandon a proof prematurely. Many complex proofs run to thousands of lines.
24	- Difficulty is NOT a valid reason to leave a `sorry`.
25	- Don't delegate to "the next iteration" or "another prover" if more effort could close it.
26	- Only modify the proof for your assigned task — leave unrelated proofs untouched.
27	- **Decompose**: break the proof into smaller sub-problems (following the blueprint's lemma structure when available: L1, L2, L3, …) and solve each individually until the entire goal closes.
28	
29	## Task completion criteria
30	
31	Your task is complete ONLY when ALL of:
32	
33	1. Every `sorry` in scope is replaced with a complete proof.
34	2. Zero axioms introduced.
35	3. The file compiles cleanly.
36	
37	## Never weaken the type to dodge the proof
38	
39	When the substantive type is unattainable this iter, you have one honest move: leave `sorry` with the **intended type signature** so the type itself encodes the claim. You must NOT substitute a tautologically-true type to trivialize the body. Three patterns are banned:
40	
41	- **Reflexive-iso placeholder.** Replacing a non-trivial `X ≅ Y` (or `Nonempty (X ≅ Y)`) target with `Nonempty (X ≅ X) := ⟨Iso.refl _⟩`. The type now asserts nothing; the docstring's "iter-N placeholder" disclosure does not undo the structural lie, and the blueprint's `\lean{...}` cross-reference will resolve to this hollow declaration.
42	- **`Classical.choice` body around an explicit witness.** Writing `Classical.choice (α := X) ⟨witness⟩` to dissolve `Type` vs `Prop` friction — the witness is unreachable through any unfold, so the def is indistinguishable from `Classical.choice ⟨any-other-X⟩` downstream. Either use `Classical.choose` on the existential chain (preserves the spec), or `Nonempty.some`-with-witness; or ship a typed `sorry`.
43	- **Empty-content `proof_wanted`.** `proof_wanted` discards the declaration post-elaboration — the named decl never enters the environment, so the blueprint's `\lean{...}` cross-reference breaks. Use `theorem name : T := by sorry` with the intended signature instead.
44	
45	**Litmus test**: if you `unfold` your declaration, does it expose the named substantive content (Kähler differential module, the explicit iso, …) or does it stop at `Classical.choice` / `Iso.refl _` / nothing? If the latter, the body is structurally vacuous — ship the typed `sorry` instead.
46	
47	## When infrastructure is missing
48	
49	Do NOT report "Mathlib lacks X" and stop. Before giving up:
50	
51	1. **Use the informal agent** (`.claude/tools/archon-informal-agent.py`): "Prove [goal] without using [missing infrastructure], only Mathlib." Even an imperfect sketch is valuable.
52	2. **Try the alternative** — formalize whatever the informal agent suggests.
53	3. **If you still can't**: write the alternative sketch to `informal/<theorem_name>.md` and record in your task result what you tried, why it failed, AND the alternative route you found. "I couldn't prove X, but here's approach Y that might work because Z" is far more useful than "infrastructure missing".
54	
55	When stuck more generally: break into smaller subgoals, search Mathlib more thoroughly, prove missing helpers yourself, try alternative strategies, re-read the blueprint, use Web Search for published proofs.
56	
57	**Impossibility vs difficulty**: technical difficulty → keep trying. Mathematical impossibility → immediately backtrack and document why.
58	
59	## Proof style
60	
61	- **Never modify working proofs** — if a declaration has no `sorry` and compiles, do not touch its body.
62	- Keep edits minimal; don't delete comments or change labels; don't add unrelated declarations.
63	- Helper lemmas you introduced may be modified if they turn out wrong.
64	- Add a concise comment above each helper lemma so reuse is easy.
65	- **`change` vs `show`** — `change` reshapes the goal up to defeq; `show` is purely display-level annotation. Using `show` where `change` is needed produces a linter warning. Default to `change` when in doubt.
66	
67	## Mathlib tags in PROGRESS.md
68	
69	The plan agent tags suggested lemmas:
70	
71	- `[verified]` — confirmed to exist.
72	- `[expected]` — guessed by naming convention. Quick `lean_local_search`; pivot if it doesn't exist.
73	- `[gap]` — verified NOT in Mathlib. Don't waste search time; formalize a workaround.
74	
75	## LSP MCP tools
76	
77	The `archon-lean-lsp` server exposes Lean LSP operations as **MCP tool calls** (full names like `mcp__archon-lean-lsp__lean_goal` / `mcp__archon-lean-lsp__lean_diagnostic_messages`). The lean4 skill reference uses short names (`lean_goal`, `lean_local_search`, …) — same tools, NOT shell binaries.
78	
79	- Always invoke through your tool-call interface, the same way as `Read` or `Grep`.
80	- **Never** call `Bash` with `lean_goal …` etc. — there is no such shell command.
81	- First LSP action: `mcp__archon-lean-lsp__lean_diagnostic_messages` on your file. If it returns `success: false` the server is cold; retry once or run `lake build` once via Bash, then retry.
82	
83	## Search protocol
84	
85	Follow `references/lean-lsp-tools-api.md`. Key priority:
86	
87	1. `lean_local_search` first.
88	2. `lean_leansearch` for semantic search — describe the mathematical content, not just the name.
89	3. `lean_loogle` for simple type patterns only.
90	4. Never use shell `find` / `grep` to locate Mathlib theorems.
91	
92	## Tooling traps
93	
94	- **Trust `goals_after`, not just empty diagnostics**: in `lean_multi_attempt`, `diagnostics: []` can be misleading (line-scoping quirks attach errors to the enclosing `by` block). Check that `goals_after` is empty or has advanced.
95	- **Beware `lean_run_code` with imports**: standalone snippets can silently swallow elaboration errors when they contain imports. Rely on the actual file's `lean_diagnostic_messages` for authoritative verification.
96	
97	## Logging
98	
99	Write to `task_results/<your_file>.md` (mirror your `.lean` path: `Algebra/WLocal.lean` → `task_results/Algebra_WLocal.lean.md`):
100	
101	```markdown
102	# Algebra/WLocal.lean
103	
104	## wLocal_iff (line 45)
105	### Attempt 1
106	- **Approach:** Direct case split on maximal ideals
107	- **Result:** FAILED — needed IsLocalRing instance, not available
108	- **Dead end:** direct case split without IsLocalRing
109	
110	### Attempt 2
111	- **Approach:** Stacks 0A31, characterize via bijection on spectra
112	- **Result:** RESOLVED
113	- **Key insight:** `PrimeSpectrum.comap_injective` bridges the gap
114	
115	## helper_bijective (line 78)
116	### Attempt 1
117	- **Approach:** Split into injectivity + surjectivity; prove injectivity
118	- **Result:** PARTIAL (branch closed) — injectivity closed; surjectivity remains
119	- **Next step:** `PrimeSpectrum.range_comap_of_surjective` for the right branch
120	- **Lemmas found:** `PrimeSpectrum.comap_surjective`
121	```
122	
123	**Rules**: one section per theorem/lemma in your file. Each attempt records approach, result (RESOLVED / FAILED / PARTIAL / IN PROGRESS), dead-end warnings or next steps. Log negative search results ("Searched 'projective module infinite rank' — nothing in Mathlib"). The plan agent merges these into `task_pending.md` / `task_done.md` next iter.
124	
125	**Read-only context**: read `task_pending.md` (prior attempts on your file) and `task_done.md` (when the problem resembles a completed one). Do not write to either.
126	
127	## End-of-session handoff
128	
129	Before stopping:
130	
131	1. Write `task_results/<your_file>.md` with: current result, lemmas discovered, concrete next step, dead-end warnings.
132	2. Save all changes; ensure the file compiles (use scoped `sorry` for stuck parts if needed).

9	# Relative spectrum of a quasi-coherent sheaf of algebras (A.1.a)
10	
11	This file is the **A.1.a** file-skeleton sub-build chapter for the project's
12	positive-genus arm of `nonempty_jacobianWitness`. It packages the relative-spectrum
13	functor `Spec_X(𝒜) : QcohAlg(X)^op ⥤ Sch/X` used by the relative Picard functor on a
14	product `C ×_k T`.
15	
16	## Status (iter-179 Block A — Mathlib `relativeGluingData` adopted)
17	
18	iter-173 Lane B scaffolded the six pinned declarations with `sorry` bodies and
19	a type-level `sorry` on `QcohAlgebra`. iter-174 Lane G replaced the type-level
20	`sorry` on `QcohAlgebra` with the **Encoding I** structure (sheafified
21	`Under`-object form: `sheaf` + `unit`). iter-176 closed the body of
22	`RelativeSpec`/`structureMorphism` with the silently-discarding placeholder
23	`RelativeSpec _𝒜 := X`, `structureMorphism _ := 𝟙 X`; the lean-auditor iter-177
24	flagged both CRITICAL "weakened-wrong". The iter-178 mathlib-analogist consult
25	(`analogies/relative-spec-encoding.md`) identified that **Mathlib already ships
26	the construction** under a different name —
27	`Scheme.AffineZariskiSite.relativeGluingData`
28	(`Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry/Sites/SmallAffineZariski.lean:293`).
29	
30	**iter-179 Block A** lands the carrier upgrade: `QcohAlgebra` gains the
31	third `coequifibered` field (Stacks 01LL form of quasi-coherence — strictly
32	weaker than `SheafOfModules.IsQuasicoherent` and provably equivalent under
33	the dense-subsite equivalence `AffineZariskiSite.sheafEquiv`), and the bodies
34	of `RelativeSpec` and `RelativeSpec.structureMorphism` are now the Mathlib
35	canonical values `(relativeGluingData _).glued` and `.toBase`. Each of the
36	three downstream theorems (`UniversalProperty`, `affine_base_iff`,
37	`base_change`) previously discharged the trivialized goal against the
38	placeholder; they now carry honest `sorry` bodies pending iter-179+ Block B
39	rewrites against `RelativeGluingData.cover` /
40	`HasAffineProperty.iff_of_iSup_eq_top` /
41	`SmallAffineZariski.isColimitCocone` (see consult Block B).
42	
43	The 6 pinned declarations are:
44	
45	1. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.QcohAlgebra` (**structure**, Block A iter-179)
46	   — a sheaf of commutative rings on `X`, an `O_X`-algebra unit
47	   `X.sheaf ⟶ sheaf`, and the Stacks-01LL `Coequifibered` overlay consumed
48	   by `relativeGluingData`. See `analogies/relative-spec-encoding.md`
49	   Decision 2.
50	2. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.RelativeSpec` (noncomputable def, ~3 LOC body)
51	   — the relative-spectrum scheme,
52	   `(AffineZariskiSite.relativeGluingData 𝒜.coequifibered).glued`.
53	3. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.RelativeSpec.UniversalProperty` (theorem, ~15 LOC)
54	   — the structure morphism `Spec_X(𝒜) → X` is an affine morphism; this is the
55	     substantive consequence of the representability statement of Stacks 01LQ.
56	4. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.RelativeSpec.affine_base_iff` (theorem, ~8 LOC)
57	   — when `X = Spec R` the relative spectrum is affine (Stacks 01LO).
58	5. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.RelativeSpec.base_change` (theorem, ~10 LOC)
59	   — `RelativeSpec` commutes with base change (Stacks 01LS).
60	6. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.RelativeSpec.functor` (def, ~8 LOC)
61	   — the object-level functorial assignment `QcohAlg(X) → Over X`.
62	
63	## Note on type expressivity
64	
65	With Lane G landed, `QcohAlgebra X` carries a non-tautological structure
66	(sheaf-of-CommRings + unit). The remaining 5 sorry bodies still encode each
67	theorem by its *intended substantive consequence* (e.g. the universal property
68	is encoded as "the structure morphism is affine", which the representability
69	statement of Stacks 01LQ structurally implies; base change is encoded as an
70	existential on the pulled-back algebra). Following the project rule "Never
71	weaken the type to dodge the proof", the litmus test for each declaration is
72	that unfolding it reveals a non-tautological claim, not `Iso.refl _` or
73	`trivial`. iter-175+ will refine `UniversalProperty` to a
74	`CategoryTheory.Functor.RepresentableBy` witness once the
75	`O_X`-algebra Hom-set is wired up via the under-category form
76	`Under X.sheaf ⊆ TopCat.Sheaf CommRingCat X`.

96	/-! ## §1. Quasi-coherent sheaves of `O_X`-algebras
97	
98	For a scheme `X`, a quasi-coherent sheaf of `O_X`-algebras is a sheaf of
99	`O_X`-algebras whose underlying `O_X`-module is quasi-coherent. Stacks tag
100	01LL packages the notion as a sheaf $\mathcal{A}$ on $X$ taking values in
101	commutative rings together with a unit map from the structure sheaf
102	$\mathcal{O}_X$, plus the quasi-coherence requirement.
103	
104	iter-179 (Block A) packages this as a triple of (i) a sheaf of CommRings,
105	(ii) an `O_X`-algebra unit from the structure sheaf, and (iii) the
106	`Coequifibered` overlay (Stacks 01LL form): the affine restriction of the
107	unit is `NatTrans.Coequifibered`, i.e. on every affine open `U` and section
108	`f`, the restriction-to-basic-open `D(f) ⊆ U` is `IsLocalization.Away f`.
109	This is the strictly-weaker, sheafified-tensor-free formulation that
110	Mathlib's `Scheme.AffineZariskiSite.relativeGluingData` consumes
111	(`Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry/Sites/SmallAffineZariski.lean:293`); it is
112	equivalent to the full `SheafOfModules.IsQuasicoherent` predicate under the
113	dense-subsite equivalence `AffineZariskiSite.sheafEquiv`. See
114	`analogies/relative-spec-encoding.md` for the iter-178 consult that
115	identified this idiom.
116	
117	Blueprint reference: `def:qc_sheaf_of_algebras` (Stacks 01LL,
118	situation-relative-spec). -/
119	
120	/-- A **quasi-coherent sheaf of `O_X`-algebras** (iter-179 Block A,
121	Mathlib-aligned form).
122	
123	A triple of
124	- `sheaf` : a sheaf of commutative rings on the underlying topological space
125	  of `X`,
126	- `unit` : an `O_X`-algebra unit, i.e. a morphism of sheaves of commutative
127	  rings `X.sheaf ⟶ sheaf` from the structure sheaf to the carrier, and
128	- `coequifibered` : the Stacks-01LL form of quasi-coherence — every
129	  restriction of `sheaf` to a basic-open `D(f) ⊆ U` is the
130	  `IsLocalization.Away f` of `sheaf U`. This is the exact predicate
131	  consumed by `Scheme.AffineZariskiSite.relativeGluingData`
132	  (`Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry/Sites/SmallAffineZariski.lean:293`).
133	
134	The shape matches the input of Mathlib's relative-gluing construction
135	verbatim, so `RelativeSpec` is defined directly as
136	`(relativeGluingData 𝒜.coequifibered).glued`. The
137	`NatTrans.Coequifibered` predicate is strictly weaker than the full
138	sheaf-of-modules quasi-coherence predicate
139	`SheafOfModules.IsQuasicoherent`, but equivalent under the dense-subsite
140	equivalence `AffineZariskiSite.sheafEquiv`, so no information is lost. See
141	`analogies/relative-spec-encoding.md` Decision 2 for the consult that
142	identified this carrier shape. -/
143	structure QcohAlgebra (X : Scheme.{u}) where
144	  /-- The underlying sheaf of commutative rings on `X`. -/
145	  sheaf : TopCat.Sheaf CommRingCat.{u} X.toPresheafedSpace
146	  /-- The `O_X`-algebra unit `X.sheaf ⟶ sheaf` exhibiting `sheaf` as a sheaf
147	  of `O_X`-algebras. -/
148	  unit : X.sheaf ⟶ sheaf
149	  /-- **Stacks 01LL quasi-coherence overlay (`Coequifibered` form)**: every
150	  restriction of `sheaf` to a basic-open `D(f) ⊆ U` is `IsLocalization.Away f`.
151	  Strictly weaker than `SheafOfModules.IsQuasicoherent` (which needs
152	  sheafified-tensor infrastructure not yet in Mathlib); equivalent under the
153	  dense-subsite equivalence `Scheme.AffineZariskiSite.sheafEquiv`. This is the
154	  exact predicate consumed by `AffineZariskiSite.relativeGluingData`
155	  (`Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry/Sites/SmallAffineZariski.lean:293`); see
156	  `analogies/relative-spec-encoding.md` Decision 2. -/
157	  coequifibered : NatTrans.Coequifibered
158	    (Functor.whiskerLeft (AffineZariskiSite.toOpensFunctor X).op unit.hom)
159	
160	namespace RelativeSpec
161	
162	/-! ## §2. The relative-spectrum scheme
163	
164	The construction proceeds affine-locally: on an affine open `U = Spec R ⊆ X` we
165	set `π⁻¹(U) := Spec(𝒜(U))`, where `𝒜(U)` is regarded as an `R`-algebra. The local
166	pieces glue compatibly because `𝒜` is quasi-coherent (the transition isomorphism
167	`𝒜(U) ⊗_R S ≅ 𝒜(V)` for `V = Spec S ⊆ U` gives an open immersion of the
168	corresponding Specs).
169	
170	Blueprint reference: `thm:relative_spec_exists` (Stacks 01LQ
171	lemma-glue-relative-spec). -/
172	
173	end RelativeSpec
174	
175	/-- The **relative spectrum** scheme `Spec_X(𝒜)` of a quasi-coherent sheaf of
176	`O_X`-algebras `𝒜`.
177	
178	**iter-179 body (Block A)**: built as the canonical Mathlib value
179	`(AffineZariskiSite.relativeGluingData 𝒜.coequifibered).glued`
180	(`Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry/RelativeGluing.lean:102`,
181	`Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry/Sites/SmallAffineZariski.lean:293`). The
182	`coequifibered` field of `QcohAlgebra` is precisely the Stacks-01LL form of
183	quasi-coherence that Mathlib's `relativeGluingData` consumes: the affine
184	restriction of the `O_X`-algebra unit is `NatTrans.Coequifibered`, i.e. every
185	basic-open restriction is an `IsLocalization.Away`. The construction glues the
186	affine pieces `Spec(𝒜(U))` along the directed affine open cover
187	`Scheme.AffineZariskiSite.directedCover X` and is the exact Mathlib-aligned
188	template used in `Hom.normalization`
189	(`Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry/Normalization.lean:120`); see
190	`analogies/relative-spec-encoding.md` for the consult that identified this
191	idiom. -/
192	noncomputable def RelativeSpec {X : Scheme.{u}} (𝒜 : X.QcohAlgebra) : Scheme.{u} :=
193	  (AffineZariskiSite.relativeGluingData 𝒜.coequifibered).glued
194	
195	/-- The **structure morphism** `π : Spec_X(𝒜) → X` of the relative spectrum.
196	
197	This auxiliary declaration (not in the 6 blueprint pins) is needed to express
198	the intended substantive types of `UniversalProperty`, `base_change`, and
199	`functor` — they all reference the structure morphism.
200	
201	**iter-179 body (Block A)**: built as the canonical Mathlib value
202	`(AffineZariskiSite.relativeGluingData 𝒜.coequifibered).toBase`
203	(`Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry/RelativeGluing.lean:114`). The map is the colimit
204	descent of the natural transformation `Spec(𝒜(U)) → U` over the directed
205	affine open cover; see `analogies/relative-spec-encoding.md` Decision 3.
206	
207	Blueprint reference: implicit in `thm:relative_spec_exists`. -/
208	noncomputable def RelativeSpec.structureMorphism {X : Scheme.{u}}
209	    (𝒜 : X.QcohAlgebra) : X.RelativeSpec 𝒜 ⟶ X :=
210	  (AffineZariskiSite.relativeGluingData 𝒜.coequifibered).toBase
211	
212	/-- **Base change of a quasi-coherent algebra (pushforward formulation,
213	iter-179 Lane B helper).**
214	
215	Given a morphism `g : T ⟶ X` and a quasi-coherent `O_X`-algebra `𝒜`, the
216	pulled-back qcoh algebra `g^* 𝒜 : T.QcohAlgebra` is realised as the
217	pushforward of the structure sheaf of the relative-spec pullback. The
218	`sheaf` and `unit` fields are concrete (the topological pushforward of the
219	pullback's structure sheaf and the canonical natural transformation `q.c`
220	respectively, where `q := pullback.fst g (structureMorphism 𝒜)`). The
221	`coequifibered` field is the Mathlib-gap claim that pushforward along an
222	affine morphism preserves the Stacks-01LL Coequifibered overlay
223	(Stacks 01LR pullback compatibility): `q` is affine via
224	`isAffineHom_isStableUnderBaseChange` applied to `UniversalProperty 𝒜`, so
225	on every affine `U ⊆ T` with section `f ∈ Γ(T, U)` we have
226	`Γ(P, q⁻¹(D(f))) = Γ(P, q⁻¹U)[1/q.app f]`, i.e. the basic-open restriction
227	of the pushforward is the relevant `IsLocalization.Away`. iter-180+ work
228	discharges this typed `sorry`. -/
229	noncomputable def QcohAlgebra.pullback {X T : Scheme.{u}} (g : T ⟶ X)
230	    (𝒜 : X.QcohAlgebra) : T.QcohAlgebra :=
231	  let q : CategoryTheory.Limits.pullback g (RelativeSpec.structureMorphism 𝒜) ⟶ T :=
232	    CategoryTheory.Limits.pullback.fst g (RelativeSpec.structureMorphism 𝒜)
233	  { sheaf := (TopCat.Sheaf.pushforward CommRingCat.{u} q.base).obj
234	      (CategoryTheory.Limits.pullback g (RelativeSpec.structureMorphism 𝒜)).sheaf
235	    unit := ⟨q.c⟩
236	    coequifibered := sorry }
237	
238	namespace RelativeSpec
239	
240	/-! ## §3. Universal property — affine structure morphism
241	
242	The Stacks 01LQ universal property says `Spec_X(𝒜)` represents the functor
243	sending an `X`-scheme `g : T → X` to the set of `O_X`-algebra maps
244	`𝒜 → g_* O_T`. A direct structural consequence of representability is that the
245	structure morphism `π : Spec_X(𝒜) → X` is *affine* (Stacks 01LR
246	lemma-spec-properties, immediate corollary). For the iter-173 file-skeleton we
247	encode the universal property by this affine-morphism consequence — the
248	substantive content is the same up to body unfolding, and the type is
249	non-tautological.
250	
251	iter-174+: refine the signature to a `CategoryTheory.Functor.RepresentableBy`
252	witness against the functor of `O_X`-algebra maps once `QcohAlgebra` is
253	unpacked and the Hom-set on algebras is available.
254	
255	Blueprint reference: `thm:relative_spec_univ` (Stacks 01LQ lemma-spec). -/
256	
257	/-- **Universal property of the relative spectrum (affine-structure form).**
258	
259	The structure morphism `π : Spec_X(𝒜) → X` of the relative spectrum is affine.
260	This is the substantive consequence of the Stacks 01LQ representability
261	statement (an `X`-scheme is the relative spectrum of some quasi-coherent
262	algebra iff its structure morphism is affine).
263	
264	iter-174+: refine the type signature to the full Yoneda-bijection statement
265	`Hom_X(T, Spec_X(𝒜)) ≃ Hom_{O_X-alg}(𝒜, g_* O_T)` once `QcohAlgebra` is
266	unpacked and an `O_X`-algebra Hom-set is in scope. The current type is the
267	non-tautological structural consequence used downstream by `affine_base_iff`
268	and `base_change`. -/
269	theorem UniversalProperty {X : Scheme.{u}} (𝒜 : X.QcohAlgebra) :
270	    IsAffineHom (RelativeSpec.structureMorphism 𝒜) := by
271	  -- Mathlib `relativeGluingData` builder; per `analogies/relative-spec-encoding.md`
272	  -- Block B. We invoke `isAffineHom_of_forall_exists_isAffineOpen`: for each `x : X`
273	  -- pick an affine open `U ∋ x` (every `X` has such by `exists_isAffineOpen_mem_and_subset`),
274	  -- and identify the structure-morphism preimage of `U` with the range of the colimit
275	  -- inclusion of the affine fiber `Spec(𝒜(U))` (via
276	  -- `Cover.RelativeGluingData.toBase_preimage_eq_opensRange_ι`); affineness of the
277	  -- opens-range then follows from `isAffineOpen_opensRange` since the source is
278	  -- `Scheme.Spec` of `𝒜.sheaf.val.obj (op U)`, an affine scheme.
279	  apply isAffineHom_of_forall_exists_isAffineOpen
280	  intro x
281	  obtain ⟨U, hU, hxU, _⟩ :=
282	    exists_isAffineOpen_mem_and_subset (X := X) (U := ⊤) (Set.mem_univ x)
283	  refine ⟨U, hxU, hU, ?_⟩
284	  set d := AffineZariskiSite.relativeGluingData 𝒜.coequifibered
285	  let i : X.AffineZariskiSite := ⟨U, hU⟩
286	  change IsAffineOpen (d.toBase ⁻¹ᵁ U)
287	  have key : d.toBase ⁻¹ᵁ U = (d.cover.f i).opensRange := by
288	    have h := d.toBase_preimage_eq_opensRange_ι i
289	    simpa [Scheme.Opens.opensRange_ι] using h
290	  rw [key]
291	  have : IsAffine (d.cover.X i) := by
292	    change IsAffine (Scheme.Spec.obj _)
293	    infer_instance
294	  exact isAffineOpen_opensRange _
295	
296	/-! ## §4. Affine base case
297	
298	When the base `X = Spec R` is affine, `Spec_X(𝒜)` reduces to the absolute
299	spectrum of the global sections: `Spec_X(𝒜) ≅ Spec(Γ(X, 𝒜))`. The substantive
300	content for the iter-173 scaffold is that the relative spectrum is itself
301	affine. This is Stacks 01LO lemma-spec-affine.
302	
303	Blueprint reference: `thm:relative_spec_affine_base`. -/
304	
305	/-- **Affine-base reduction of the relative spectrum.**
306	
307	For an affine scheme `X = Spec R` and a quasi-coherent `O_X`-algebra `𝒜`,
308	the relative spectrum `Spec_X(𝒜)` is itself an affine scheme. (More precisely,
309	there is a canonical isomorphism `Spec_X(𝒜) ≅ Spec(Γ(X, 𝒜))`, but extracting
310	`Γ(X, 𝒜) : CommRingCat` requires the unpacked structure of `QcohAlgebra`,
311	which is iter-174+ work.)
312	
313	iter-174+: refine to the full statement
314	`Nonempty ((Spec R).RelativeSpec 𝒜 ≅ Spec (Γ((Spec R), 𝒜)))`
315	once `Γ` for `QcohAlgebra` is in scope. -/
316	theorem affine_base_iff {R : CommRingCat.{u}} (𝒜 : (Spec R).QcohAlgebra) :
317	    IsAffine ((Spec R).RelativeSpec 𝒜) := by
318	  -- Affineness of the relative-spec total space when the base is affine is the
319	  -- standard "affine over affine is affine" consequence: by
320	  -- `UniversalProperty` the structure morphism is `IsAffineHom`, and `Spec R`
321	  -- is itself `IsAffine`, so `isAffine_of_isAffineHom` closes the goal.
322	  have h : IsAffineHom (RelativeSpec.structureMorphism 𝒜) := UniversalProperty 𝒜
323	  exact isAffine_of_isAffineHom (RelativeSpec.structureMorphism 𝒜)
324	
325	/-! ## §5. Base change
326	
327	The relative spectrum commutes with base change: for a morphism `g : T → X`
328	and a quasi-coherent `O_X`-algebra `𝒜`, the pullback `g^* 𝒜` is a
329	quasi-coherent `O_T`-algebra and
330	`T ×_X Spec_X(𝒜) ≅ Spec_T(g^* 𝒜)`.
331	
332	Blueprint reference: `thm:relative_spec_base_change` (Stacks 01LS
333	lemma-spec-base-change). -/
334	
335	/-! ### Helper for `base_change` (iter-179 Lane B)
336	
337	The base-change theorem packages the canonical iso content into a named helper
338	(`pullback_iso`, below) and consumes the file-level
339	`Scheme.QcohAlgebra.pullback` constructor declared just above the namespace.
340	Together they fill the iter-179 helper budget = 2; `base_change` itself is a
341	1-line existential witness. -/
342	
343	/-- **Canonical base-change isomorphism.** The pullback
344	`pullback g (structureMorphism 𝒜)` (formed in `Scheme`) is canonically
345	isomorphic to the relative spectrum of the pulled-back qcoh algebra
346	`QcohAlgebra.pullback g 𝒜`. The iso descends from the universal property of
347	`Cover.RelativeGluingData.glued` applied to the directed affine cover of
348	`T`: each affine open `U ⊆ T` has preimage `q⁻¹U` affine (since `q` is the
349	projection from the relative-spec pullback and is affine), and these
350	affine pieces are Mathlib-isomorphic to `Spec(g^*𝒜)(U)` by construction.
351	Iter-180+ work: assemble the cocone explicitly and invoke the colimit
352	universal property. -/
353	theorem pullback_iso {X T : Scheme.{u}} (g : T ⟶ X)
354	    (𝒜 : X.QcohAlgebra) :
355	    Nonempty (CategoryTheory.Limits.pullback g (RelativeSpec.structureMorphism 𝒜) ≅
356	              T.RelativeSpec (QcohAlgebra.pullback g 𝒜)) :=
357	  sorry
358	
359	/-- **Base change of the relative spectrum.**
360	
361	For a morphism `g : T → X` and a quasi-coherent `O_X`-algebra `𝒜`, there
362	exists a quasi-coherent `O_T`-algebra `𝒜' = g^* 𝒜` and a canonical isomorphism
363	of `T`-schemes `T ×_X Spec_X(𝒜) ≅ Spec_T(g^* 𝒜)`.
364	
365	iter-179 body (Block B): witnessed by the named helpers `QcohAlgebra.pullback`
366	and `pullback_iso`. -/
367	theorem base_change {X T : Scheme.{u}} (g : T ⟶ X) (_𝒜 : X.QcohAlgebra) :
368	    ∃ (𝒜' : T.QcohAlgebra),
369	      Nonempty (pullback g (RelativeSpec.structureMorphism _𝒜) ≅
370	                  T.RelativeSpec 𝒜') :=
371	  ⟨QcohAlgebra.pullback g _𝒜, pullback_iso g _𝒜⟩
372	
373	/-! ## §6. Functoriality
374	
375	The construction `𝒜 ↦ Spec_X(𝒜)` extends to a contravariant functor
376	`Spec_X : QcohAlg(X)^op ⥤ Over X`. The iter-173 file-skeleton encodes the
377	object-level functorial assignment as `X.QcohAlgebra → Over X`; the
378	morphism-level action and full `Functor` packaging are iter-174+ work after
379	`QcohAlgebra` is unpacked to a category.
380	
381	Blueprint reference: `thm:relative_spec_functorial` (Stacks 01LR
382	definition-relative-spec + lemma-glueing-gives-functor-spec). -/
383	
384	/-- **The relative-spectrum functor (object level).**
385	
386	The object-level functorial action `𝒜 ↦ Over.mk (π_𝒜) : Over X`, packaging
387	the relative spectrum together with its structure morphism over `X`.
388	
389	iter-174+: the body is concrete via `Over.mk (RelativeSpec.structureMorphism 𝒜)`
390	but is left as `sorry` here because `RelativeSpec.structureMorphism` is itself
391	a typed `sorry`; once the structure morphism lands the body collapses to
392	`fun 𝒜 => Over.mk (structureMorphism 𝒜)`. The full categorical functor
393	`X.QcohAlgebra ⥤ Over X` (with `map` action induced by the universal property)
394	becomes expressible once `QcohAlgebra` carries its category structure. -/
395	noncomputable def functor (X : Scheme.{u}) :
396	    X.QcohAlgebra → Over X :=
397	  fun 𝒜 => Over.mk (RelativeSpec.structureMorphism 𝒜)
398	
399	end RelativeSpec
400	
401	end Scheme
402	
403	end AlgebraicGeometry
